Cook lentil pasta according to package instructions (usually 8-10 minutes)
While pasta cooks, blend ripe avocado with lactose-free cream, salt, and pepper until smooth
Drain pasta and mix immediately with the creamy avocado sauce
Fold in cooked edamame beans for extra protein and texture
Serve topped with roasted cherry tomatoes and lactose-free mozzarella
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste
recipe_tips: Lentil pasta can become mushy if overcooked - check frequently
Edamame can be frozen (thawed) or fresh cooked
The avocado sauce will be slightly thicker with lactose-free cream
Add nutritional yeast for extra umami if desired
This version provides exceptional protein content for plant-based eaters
recipes.recipe_notes: Protein-packed, allergen-friendly pasta featuring lentil pasta and edamame for exceptional nutrition. Perfect for those avoiding gluten and dairy while maximizing protein intake.

recipes.name: Sweet Potato Bowl with Falafel and Feta
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Peel and cube the sweet potato into 2cm pieces
Spread sweet potato cubes on a baking tray lined with baking paper
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper
Roast in preheated oven for about 25 minutes until golden and tender
Meanwhile, cut the bell pepper and cucumber into cubes
Heat falafel briefly in a pan or oven according to package instructions
Arrange roasted sweet potatoes in serving bowl
Add warmed falafel, bell pepper cubes, cucumber, and crumbled feta cheese
Top with a generous spoonful of Greek yogurt
Season with extra salt and pepper to taste
recipe_tips: Cut sweet potato into uniform pieces for even roasting
Don't overcrowd the baking tray - sweet potatoes need space to caramelize
Store-bought falafel works perfectly, or use homemade if preferred
Crumble feta rather than cutting for better distribution
Serve immediately while sweet potatoes are still warm
recipes.recipe_notes: Mediterranean-inspired bowl combining roasted sweet potatoes with protein-rich falafel and creamy feta. The Greek yogurt adds probiotics and balances the flavors beautifully.

recipes.name: Sweet Potato Bowl with Falafel (Lactose-Free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Peel and cube the sweet potato into 2cm pieces
Spread sweet potato cubes on a baking tray lined with baking paper
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper
Roast in preheated oven for about 25 minutes until golden and tender
Meanwhile, cut the red bell pepper and cucumber into cubes
Heat falafel briefly in a pan or oven according to package instructions
Arrange roasted sweet potatoes in serving bowl
Add warmed falafel, red bell pepper cubes, cucumber, and crumbled lactose-free feta
Top with a generous spoonful of lactose-free Greek yogurt
Season with salt and pepper to taste
recipe_tips: Red bell pepper adds extra sweetness and vitamin C compared to other peppers
Lactose-free feta has similar texture and taste to regular feta
For extra protein, consider using unsweetened soy yogurt instead
Roast sweet potatoes until edges are caramelized for maximum flavor
This version is perfect for those with lactose intolerance
recipes.recipe_notes: Dairy-free Mediterranean bowl that doesn't compromise on flavor or nutrition. The lactose-free alternatives maintain the creamy textures and tangy flavors of the original.

recipes.name: Burgers with Sweet Potato Fries and Grilled Vegetables
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Cut sweet potatoes into wedges, toss with olive oil, salt, and pepper
Roast sweet potato wedges for about 25 minutes until golden and tender
If using fresh mince: mix beef with garlic, beaten egg, and breadcrumbs, then shape into patty
If using pre-made burger: season lightly with garlic and cook directly
Grill or pan-fry the burger patty until cooked through (internal temp 70°C)
Meanwhile, grill eggplant and zucchini slices until tender and slightly charred
Toast or warm the burger bun
Assemble burger with patty, grilled vegetables, bean sprouts, and fresh salad
Serve with roasted sweet potato fries on the side
recipe_tips: Don't press down on burger while cooking - it releases juices
Let burger rest for 2-3 minutes after cooking for better texture
Sweet potato wedges get crispier if not overcrowded on the tray
Grill vegetables until they have nice char marks for extra flavor
Toast bun lightly for better texture and to prevent sogginess
recipes.recipe_notes: Classic comfort food elevated with sweet potato fries and grilled vegetables. The combination provides complete nutrition while satisfying burger cravings.

recipes.name: Beyond Meat Burgers with Sweet Potato Fries (Gluten-Free & Lactose-Free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Cut sweet potatoes into wedges, toss with olive oil, salt, and pepper
Roast sweet potato wedges for about 25 minutes until golden and tender
If using Beyond Meat mince: mix with garlic and gluten-free breadcrumbs, shape into patty
If using pre-made Beyond Meat patty: cook directly
Cook Beyond Meat patty in a pan with a little oil until golden and heated through
Meanwhile, grill eggplant and zucchini slices until tender
Toast the gluten-free bun until lightly golden
Assemble burger with Beyond Meat patty, grilled vegetables, bean sprouts, and salad
Serve with roasted sweet potato fries
recipe_tips: Beyond Meat patties cook faster than beef - don't overcook
Gluten-free buns can be more delicate - toast gently
Let Beyond Meat patty rest briefly after cooking for best texture
Bean sprouts add fresh crunch - add them just before serving
This version provides excellent plant-based protein satisfaction
recipes.recipe_notes: Plant-based burger that delivers on taste and nutrition. Beyond Meat provides complete protein while accommodating gluten-free and lactose-free dietary needs.

recipes.name: Plant-Based Chocolate Mousse with Rice Cakes
instructions: Prepare espresso or dissolve 1 tsp instant coffee in 50ml hot water
Soak both rice cakes in the espresso until softened but not falling apart
In a bowl, mix protein powder, raw cacao powder, and psyllium husk
Gradually add unsweetened plant milk while whisking until mixture becomes mousse-thick
Let mousse sit for 2-3 minutes to allow psyllium to thicken further
Place first espresso-soaked rice cake in serving bowl or glass
Spread half the chocolate mousse over the first rice cake
Add second rice cake on top
Top with remaining mousse
Sprinkle cacao nibs generously on top for crunch and extra antioxidants
recipe_tips: Don't over-soak rice cakes - they should hold their shape but be coffee-infused
Psyllium husk continues to thicken, so work quickly once mixed
For extra richness, use slightly warm plant milk
Cacao nibs add essential crunch - don't skip them
Can be made 30 minutes ahead and chilled for firmer texture
recipes.recipe_notes: Revolutionary plant-based dessert that delivers restaurant-quality satisfaction with incredible nutrition stats. The rice cake base soaked in espresso creates an unexpected tiramisu-like experience.

recipes.name: Blood Sugar Friendly Buddha Bowl with Quinoa
instructions: Rinse quinoa and cook in 160ml water for 15 minutes until fluffy
Roast sweet potato cubes at 200°C for 20 minutes until tender
Steam broccoli for 5 minutes until bright green
Warm black beans and season with salt and pepper
Make dressing by whisking tahini, lemon juice, apple cider vinegar, garlic, and olive oil
Arrange quinoa as base in bowl
Top with black beans, roasted sweet potato, steamed broccoli, and fresh spinach
Add sliced avocado and sprinkle with hemp hearts and chia seeds
Drizzle with tahini dressing and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Cook quinoa with a bay leaf for extra flavor
Chia seeds add crunch and additional fiber
Sweet potato provides slow-releasing natural sugars
This meal keeps blood sugar stable for 4-5 hours
recipes.recipe_notes: Specifically designed for blood sugar management with slow carbs, high fiber, and optimal protein-fat balance for sustained energy.

recipes.name: Slow Carb Lentil & Brown Rice Power Bowl
instructions: Cook brown rice in 120ml water for 30-35 minutes until tender
Halve Brussels sprouts and roast at 200°C for 20 minutes until crispy
Steam kale for 3-4 minutes until wilted but still bright green
Warm cooked lentils with turmeric and garlic
Make dressing by whisking olive oil, balsamic vinegar, and Dijon mustard
Layer brown rice as base in bowl
Arrange lentils, roasted Brussels sprouts, steamed kale, and grated carrots
Sprinkle with sunflower seeds and ground flaxseed
Drizzle with dressing and garnish with fresh herbs

recipes.name: Brussels Sprouts with Sweet Potato
instructions: Clean the Brussels sprouts and remove outer leaves, then blanch them in boiling water for 3-4 minutes
Peel sweet potatoes, cut into chunks, and cook in boiling salted water until tender (15-20 minutes)
Meanwhile, dice the onion and heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat
Fry the diced onion until softened, then add the blanched Brussels sprouts
Season Brussels sprouts and onion with salt and pepper, cook for 5-8 minutes until lightly browned
In a dry pan, lightly roast the walnuts for 2-3 minutes until fragrant
Drain the sweet potatoes and mash until smooth, gradually adding oat milk and mustard
Season the mashed sweet potatoes with salt and pepper to taste
Fold the sautéed Brussels sprouts and onion into the mashed sweet potatoes
Serve topped with crumbled old cheese and roasted walnuts
Optional: garnish with chopped sun-dried tomatoes for extra flavor
recipe_tips: Don't overcook Brussels sprouts - they should retain some bite
Roasting the walnuts enhances their nutty flavor significantly
The mustard adds a nice tangy depth to the mashed sweet potatoes
Can be made ahead and reheated - add cheese and walnuts just before serving
recipes.recipe_notes: Hearty comfort food combining the sweetness of mashed sweet potatoes with the earthy flavor of Brussels sprouts, topped with creamy cheese and crunchy walnuts.

recipes.name: Nutritious Tuna Poke Bowl with Edamame
instructions: If using raw tuna, ensure it's sashimi-grade and cut into cubes
If using cooked tuna, flake or cube the tuna and set aside
Steam or boil edamame beans for 3-4 minutes if frozen, then shell them
Slice cucumber into rounds or half-moons
Cut avocado in half, remove pit, and slice
Break rice cakes into bite-sized crispy pieces
Tear nori sheets into smaller pieces
Make dressing by whisking sesame oil, soy sauce, and rice vinegar
Arrange all ingredients in a bowl: tuna, edamame, cucumber, and avocado
Top with crispy rice pieces and torn nori
Drizzle with dressing and sprinkle with sesame seeds
Season with salt and pepper to taste, serve immediately
recipe_tips: Use sashimi-grade tuna for the freshest flavor if eating raw
Edamame can be prepared in advance and stored in the fridge
Rice cakes add great texture - you can also use puffed rice
Nori adds umami flavor and important minerals
This bowl is perfect for meal prep - keep dressing separate until serving
recipes.recipe_notes: Nutrient-dense poke-style bowl combining high-quality protein from tuna with plant-based protein from edamame, healthy fats from avocado, and the mineral benefits of sea vegetables.

recipes.name: Vegetarian Protein Poke Bowl with Marinated Tofu
instructions: Press tofu to remove excess water, then cube into bite-sized pieces
Marinate tofu cubes in soy sauce, ginger, and garlic powder for 10 minutes
Steam or boil edamame beans for 3-4 minutes if frozen, then shell them
Slice cucumber into rounds or half-moons
Cut avocado in half, remove pit, and slice
Break rice cakes into bite-sized crispy pieces
Tear nori sheets into smaller pieces
Make dressing by whisking sesame oil, remaining soy sauce, and rice vinegar
Arrange all ingredients in a bowl: marinated tofu, edamame, cucumber, and avocado
Top with crispy rice pieces, torn nori, and hemp hearts
Drizzle with dressing and sprinkle with sesame seeds
Season with salt and pepper to taste, serve immediately
recipe_tips: Press tofu for 15 minutes before marinating for better texture
You can pan-fry the marinated tofu for 2-3 minutes for extra flavor
Hemp hearts add a nutty flavor and boost the omega-3 content
Edamame can be prepared in advance and stored in the fridge
This bowl is perfect for meal prep - keep dressing separate until serving
recipes.recipe_notes: Plant-based version of the nutritious poke bowl with marinated tofu providing complete protein, enhanced with hemp hearts for omega-3s and additional fiber from edamame.

recipes.name: High-Protein Power Bowl with Lean Beef & Sweet Potato
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and line a baking tray
Cube sweet potato and toss with half the olive oil, salt, and paprika
Roast sweet potato for 20-25 minutes until tender and lightly caramelized
Season beef cubes with garlic powder, black pepper, and remaining salt
Heat remaining olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat
Cook beef cubes for 6-8 minutes, turning to brown all sides
Let beef rest for 2 minutes to retain juices
Slice avocado and arrange in serving bowl
Add cottage cheese to one section of the bowl
Place roasted sweet potato and cooked beef in separate sections
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Choose lean cuts like sirloin or tenderloin for best protein-to-fat ratio
Don't overcook the beef - aim for medium for optimal tenderness
Sweet potato can be batch-cooked and reheated throughout the week
Full-fat cottage cheese provides better satiety and nutrient absorption
recipes.recipe_notes: Nutrient-dense power bowl emphasizing vitamins and minerals alongside optimal macros for sustained energy and health.

recipes.name: Vegetarian High-Protein Power Bowl with Tofu & Sweet Potato
instructions: Press tofu for 15 minutes to remove excess water, then cube
Preheat oven to 200°C and line a baking tray
Cube sweet potato and toss with half the olive oil, salt, and paprika
Roast sweet potato for 20-25 minutes until tender and lightly caramelized
Season tofu cubes with garlic powder, turmeric, black pepper, and remaining salt
Heat remaining olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat
Cook tofu cubes for 8-10 minutes, turning to golden all sides
Slice avocado and arrange in serving bowl
Add cottage cheese to one section of the bowl
Place roasted sweet potato and golden tofu in separate sections
Sprinkle hemp hearts over the entire bowl
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Press tofu well for better texture and flavor absorption
Turmeric adds anti-inflammatory benefits and golden color
Hemp hearts boost protein and add pleasant nutty flavor
Choose full-fat plant-based cottage cheese alternative if avoiding dairy
recipes.recipe_notes: Plant-based power bowl maximizing vitamins, minerals, and fiber while delivering complete protein for sustained energy and health.

recipes.name: Purple Sweet Potato Toast with Vegan Feta & Scrambled Eggs
ingredients: 2 slices gluten-free purple sweet potato bread
instructions: Toast 2 slices of pre-made purple sweet potato bread until golden
While toasting, scramble 2 eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper
Mash half an avocado with lemon juice and a pinch of salt
Quickly warm the pre-made sautéed spinach and peas mixture
Spread mashed avocado evenly on both toast slices
Layer the warm spinach and peas mixture on top
Add the scrambled eggs as the next layer
Crumble vegan feta over the eggs
Sprinkle with roasted pumpkin seeds and black sesame seeds
Serve immediately while toast is still warm

recipes.name: Vegan Purple Sweet Potato Toast with Chickpea Scramble
ingredients: 2 slices gluten-free purple sweet potato bread
instructions: Toast 2 slices of pre-made purple sweet potato bread until golden
Gently warm the pre-made chickpea scramble in a pan
Mash half an avocado with lemon juice and a pinch of salt
Quickly warm the pre-made sautéed spinach and peas mixture
Spread mashed avocado evenly on both toast slices
Layer the warm spinach and peas mixture on top
Add the warmed chickpea scramble as the protein layer
Crumble vegan feta generously over the scramble
Sprinkle with roasted pumpkin seeds and black sesame seeds
Serve immediately while all components are warm

recipes.name: Crispy Tofu Bowl with Roasted Pumpkin & Green Cashew Sauce
ingredients: Small handful fresh basil or kemangi leaves
instructions: Soak cashews in water for 1-2 hours to soften
Press tofu to remove excess water, then cut into cubes
Toss tofu with soy sauce and sesame oil, let rest for 10 minutes
Preheat oven to 200°C and line a baking tray
Cube pumpkin and eggplant, toss with 1 tsp coconut oil, turmeric, coriander, and salt
Roast vegetables for 20-25 minutes until tender and lightly browned
Coat marinated tofu with cornstarch and a pinch of salt
Heat olive oil in a pan and pan-fry tofu cubes until golden and crispy (5-6 minutes)
In the same pan, heat remaining coconut oil and sauté minced garlic
Add spinach and bok choy, stir-fry quickly until just wilted
For green sauce: blend soaked cashews, lime juice, coconut milk, olive oil, basil, salt, and 1-2 tbsp water until creamy
Assemble bowl: layer roasted pumpkin and eggplant as base
Add sautéed greens, top with crispy tofu
Drizzle with green cashew sauce and garnish with fried shallots and fresh basil
recipe_tips: Press tofu well for crispier results - use a clean kitchen towel
Don't overcrowd the pan when frying tofu for maximum crispiness
Soak cashews overnight for ultra-smooth sauce texture
Roasted vegetables can be made ahead and reheated
Green sauce keeps in fridge for 2-3 days
recipes.recipe_notes: Vibrant Asian-inspired bowl featuring crispy tofu with aromatic roasted vegetables and a creamy herb-cashew sauce. Perfect balance of protein, healthy fats, and slow carbs.

recipes.name: High-Protein Sourdough Toast with Baba Ganoush, Eggs & Tofu
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat
Add sliced mushrooms, season with salt and pepper
Sauté mushrooms until golden brown and caramelized (5-7 minutes)
Finish mushrooms with lemon juice if desired, then set aside
In the same pan, either crumble tofu for a scramble-like texture or slice and sear until golden on both sides
Meanwhile, poach or boil 3 eggs to your preferred doneness
Toast the sourdough bread slice until golden and crispy
Spread 3-4 tablespoons of baba ganoush generously on the toast
Layer the sautéed mushrooms and tofu on top of the baba ganoush
Carefully place the cooked eggs on top
Sprinkle with fresh chopped herbs and mixed seeds
Season with a final pinch of salt and pepper if needed
Serve immediately while toast is still warm
recipe_tips: Use a variety of mushrooms (shiitake, cremini, oyster) for complex flavor
Don't overcrowd mushrooms in the pan - cook in batches if needed
Crumbled tofu gives a scrambled texture, sliced gives more distinct protein layers
Choose thick-cut sourdough for better structural support
Mixed seeds can be lightly toasted for extra flavor and crunch
recipes.recipe_notes: Protein powerhouse breakfast combining the tangy richness of baba ganoush with multiple protein sources. Perfect for those needing sustained energy and muscle support.

recipes.name: High-protein pistachio cheesecake
instructions: Preheat oven to 163°C (325°F)
Add all ingredients (cottage cheese, Greek yogurt, egg, pistachios, maple syrup, almond flour, and vanilla) to a blender
Blend on high speed until completely smooth with no pistachio chunks remaining
Pour the mixture into a small ramekin or individual baking dish (about 10cm/4 inches) lined with parchment paper
Bake for 15-20 minutes. The edges should feel set but the center should still have a slight jiggle when you move the dish
Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature
Transfer to the fridge and chill for at least 2-4 hours (overnight is best)
Serve chilled, optionally topped with extra crushed pistachios
recipe_tips: The center will set completely as it cools in the fridge - don't overbake
For a sweeter cheesecake, add an extra tablespoon of maple syrup
Line your ramekin with parchment paper for easy removal
Store covered in the fridge for up to 3 days
recipes.recipe_notes: Individual high-protein pistachio cheesecake that's perfect for meal prep breakfasts. The creamy texture and natural pistachio flavor make this feel like dessert while providing substantial protein to start your day.

recipes.name: Lactose-free high-protein pistachio cheesecake
instructions: Preheat oven to 163°C (325°F)
Add all ingredients (lactose-free cottage cheese, lactose-free Greek yogurt, egg, pistachios, maple syrup, almond flour, and vanilla) to a blender
Blend on high speed until completely smooth with no pistachio chunks remaining
Pour the mixture into a small ramekin or individual baking dish (about 10cm/4 inches) lined with parchment paper
Bake for 15-20 minutes. The edges should feel set but the center should still have a slight jiggle when you move the dish
Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature
Transfer to the fridge and chill for at least 2-4 hours (overnight is best)
Serve chilled, optionally topped with extra crushed pistachios
recipe_tips: The center will set completely as it cools in the fridge - don't overbake
For a sweeter cheesecake, add an extra tablespoon of maple syrup
Line your ramekin with parchment paper for easy removal
Use certified lactose-free dairy products for best results
Store covered in the fridge for up to 3 days
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free individual pistachio cheesecake that's gentle on digestion. The creamy texture and natural pistachio flavor make this feel like dessert while providing substantial protein to start your day.

recipes.name: Lactose-free cottage cheese breakfast bowl
instructions: Add lactose-free cottage cheese to a bowl
Mix in vanilla protein powder until well combined
Add frozen berries (they'll create the 'ice cream' texture)
Drizzle with honey
Top with dark chocolate chips and sliced almonds
Mix together and enjoy immediately while berries are still frozen for best texture

recipes.name: Lactose-free protein cookie dough bites
instructions: In a bowl, mix almond flour, protein powder, and salt
Add almond butter and maple syrup, mix until crumbly
Add almond milk 1 tablespoon at a time until dough forms
Fold in lactose-free chocolate chips
Roll into 4 equal-sized balls
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before eating
Store in airtight container in fridge for up to 1 week
recipe_tips: Dough should be slightly sticky but hold together - add more almond milk if too dry
Use certified lactose-free chocolate chips for completely dairy-free
Roll in cocoa powder or coconut for extra flavor
Double the batch for meal prep
recipes.recipe_notes: Viral no-bake cookie dough bites that are safe to eat and lactose-free. Perfect high-protein snack for anyone avoiding dairy.

recipes.name: Marry me butter chicken pasta
instructions: Cook whole wheat pasta according to package directions, drain and set aside
Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, cook chicken until golden and cooked through (6-8 minutes)
Remove chicken, add minced garlic to pan, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Return chicken to pan, mix well
Top with grated parmesan and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Reserve pasta water to thin sauce if needed
Use full-fat Greek yogurt for creamiest texture
Butterfly chicken breast for faster cooking
Fresh spinach adds color and nutrition without overwhelming flavor
recipes.recipe_notes: Viral 'marry me' pasta combining butter chicken flavors with Italian comfort food. This protein-packed version keeps the indulgent taste while delivering serious nutrition.

recipes.name: Gluten-free lactose-free marry me butter chicken pasta
instructions: Cook gluten-free pasta according to package directions (usually 1-2 minutes less than wheat pasta), drain and set aside
Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, cook chicken until golden and cooked through (6-8 minutes)
Remove chicken, add minced garlic to pan, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in lactose-free Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Return chicken to pan, mix well and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Gluten-free pasta can get mushy - cook al dente
Use certified gluten-free spice blends to avoid cross-contamination
Lactose-free yogurt behaves exactly like regular in this recipe
Reserve extra pasta water - gluten-free pasta absorbs more liquid
recipes.recipe_notes: Gluten-free and lactose-free version of the viral 'marry me' pasta. Perfect for those with celiac disease or lactose intolerance who don't want to miss out on trending recipes.

recipes.name: Vegetarian marry me mushroom pasta
instructions: Cook whole wheat pasta according to package directions, drain and set aside
Slice mushrooms, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, sauté mushrooms until golden and liquid evaporates (5-6 minutes)
Add drained chickpeas to pan, cook 2 minutes until slightly crispy
Add minced garlic, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Top with grated parmesan and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Don't crowd mushrooms in pan - cook in batches for best browning
Use a mix of mushroom varieties for complex flavor
Chickpeas add protein and creamy texture when mashed slightly
Fresh herbs like cilantro make great garnish
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version of viral 'marry me' pasta using hearty mushrooms and protein-rich chickpeas. All the flavor and creaminess of the original without the meat.

recipes.name: Lactose-free vegetarian cloud bread sandwich
instructions: Preheat oven to 150°C (300°F) and line baking sheet with parchment
Separate egg whites from yolks
Beat egg whites with pinch of salt until stiff peaks form
In separate bowl, mix egg yolks, lactose-free yogurt, protein powder, and baking powder
Gently fold yolk mixture into egg whites, being careful not to deflate
Spoon mixture into 2 large circles on baking sheet (about 10cm diameter each)
Bake for 18-20 minutes until golden and set
Let cool completely (they'll firm up as they cool)
Fill with mashed avocado, hummus, lettuce, and sliced tomato
recipe_tips: Don't over-mix when folding - cloudy texture comes from air in egg whites
Cloud bread is delicate when warm - let it cool before handling
Use lactose-free yogurt for completely dairy-free option
Store unfilled cloud bread in airtight container for up to 2 days
recipes.recipe_notes: Viral cloud bread recipe made lactose-free and vegetarian. Fluffy, protein-packed bread alternative that looks like clouds and is perfect for sandwiches.

recipes.name: Lactose-free cucumber sushi protein boats
instructions: Cut cucumber in half lengthwise, scoop out seeds with spoon to create 'boats'
Cut each half into 3 equal pieces (6 boats total)
In bowl, mix drained tuna, lactose-free Greek yogurt, and sriracha
Fill each cucumber boat with tuna mixture
Top with thin avocado slices
Sprinkle with sesame seeds
Garnish with nori seaweed strips
Serve with soy sauce on the side
recipe_tips: Pat cucumber dry after scooping to prevent soggy filling
Use a melon baller to easily remove cucumber seeds
Adjust sriracha to your spice preference
Make ahead and store in fridge for up to 4 hours
Swap tuna for cooked shrimp or salmon for variety
recipes.recipe_notes: Viral cucumber sushi boats that are low-carb, high-protein, and visually stunning. Lactose-free version uses yogurt instead of mayo for creamy texture.

recipes.name: Healthy sweet potato brownies
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C/390°F
Use a fork to poke a few holes into sweet potato, bake with skin on until tender (about 30-40 minutes)
Let the sweet potato cool down completely
Peel the sweet potato and add to a bowl with peanut butter, mix well until smooth
Add cacao powder, oat flour, salt, baking powder, vanilla, and sweetener - mix well
Add cold espresso shot and plant milk, mix until combined
Fold in chopped dark chocolate (make sure espresso is cold so chocolate doesn't melt)
Pour batter into a greased oven-proof dish (20x20cm recommended)
Reduce oven temperature to 176°C/350°F
Bake for 35-40 minutes until set but still fudgy in the center
Let cool completely before cutting into 12 squares
recipe_tips: Bake sweet potato ahead of time to save prep time
If you don't have oat flour, grind rolled oats in a blender
Espresso shot MUST be cold or chocolate will melt into the batter
For sweeter brownies, increase sweetener or use maple syrup
Store in airtight container for up to 5 days
Can be frozen for up to 3 months
recipes.recipe_notes: Viral sweet potato brownies that are incredibly rich and gooey. These taste like the real deal but are packed with whole food ingredients. Perfect healthy snack for meal prep!

recipes.name: Flourless carrot cake energy balls
instructions: In food processor, blend grated carrot, walnuts, dates, oats, shredded coconut, and cinnamon until you get a dense, sticky dough
Shape mixture into 24 small balls (about 20g each) and place on a parchment-lined tray
Freeze balls for 1 hour to firm up
Meanwhile, blend cashews with coconut milk (liquid part only), honey, and vanilla extract until completely smooth and creamy
Remove balls from freezer and coat each one with the cashew cream using a spoon or dipping fork
Return coated balls to tray and freeze for at least 1 more hour before serving
Store in freezer for up to 3 months, or refrigerator for up to 1 week
recipe_tips: Use fresh grated carrot for best texture and moisture
If dough is too dry, add 1-2 dates; if too wet, add more oats
Freeze balls before coating to prevent them from falling apart
Use only the thick liquid part of coconut milk, not the cream on top
Let balls sit at room temperature 5-10 minutes before eating for best texture
Can roll in extra shredded coconut instead of cashew coating for variation
recipes.recipe_notes: Viral flourless carrot cake balls that taste like dessert but are packed with whole food ingredients. No-bake recipe credited to @lindsay.keosayian. Perfect healthy snack that satisfies sweet cravings!

recipes.name: Cacao-blueberry power oats
instructions: In a pot, combine oats, buckwheat flakes, and almond milk
Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes until creamy
Remove from heat and stir in cacao powder, protein powder, cinnamon, and salt
Mix until protein powder is fully incorporated
Transfer to bowl
Top with fresh blueberries and drizzle with almond butter
Serve immediately
recipe_tips: Use frozen blueberries if fresh aren't available
Add cacao powder after cooking to preserve flavanols
Adjust liquid to desired consistency
recipes.recipe_notes: Power-packed breakfast with 85mg flavanols supporting heart and brain health

recipes.name: Cashew yogurt bowl with berries and cacao nibs
instructions: Spoon cashew yogurt into serving bowl
Top with fresh blackberries
Sprinkle cacao nibs evenly over the top
Add chia seeds
Drizzle with honey if desired
Garnish with fresh mint
Serve immediately

recipes.name: Chocolate protein smoothie
instructions: Add soy milk to blender first
Add frozen banana and blueberries
Add cocoa powder and protein powder
Add flaxseed and hemp hearts
Add a few ice cubes
Blend on high for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth
Pour into glass and serve immediately

recipes.name: Quinoa salad with apples and walnuts
instructions: Cook quinoa according to package instructions, let cool
In large bowl, combine cooled quinoa, spinach, and cooked lentils
Dice apple and add to bowl
Chop walnuts and add to salad
In small bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, and Dijon mustard
Pour dressing over salad and toss to combine
Season with salt and pepper
Serve immediately or refrigerate for meal prep
recipe_tips: Use pre-cooked quinoa for faster prep
Toast walnuts for enhanced flavor
Add apple just before serving to prevent browning
recipes.recipe_notes: Nutrient-dense salad with complete plant protein and 15mg flavanols from fresh apple

recipes.name: Tofu bowl with green vegetables and berries
instructions: Press tofu to remove excess water, then cube
Heat sesame oil in large pan over medium-high heat
Add tofu cubes and cook 8-10 minutes until golden on all sides
Add kale and zucchini, sauté for 5 minutes until tender
Season with salt and pepper
In small bowl, whisk tahini with lemon juice and 1-2 tbsp water until creamy
Transfer vegetables and tofu to serving bowl
Top with fresh strawberries and herbs
Drizzle with tahini dressing
recipe_tips: Marinate tofu beforehand for extra flavor
Use frozen berries if fresh not available
Add garlic to tahini dressing for extra flavor
recipes.recipe_notes: Colorful plant-based bowl with 10mg flavanols and complete nutrition

recipes.name: Buckwheat pasta with roasted vegetables and cacao sauce
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Toss broccoli and bell peppers with olive oil, salt, and pepper
Roast vegetables for 20-25 minutes until tender and slightly charred
Cook buckwheat pasta according to package instructions
In saucepan, warm tomato sauce with minced garlic
Stir cacao powder into sauce for depth of flavor
Drain pasta and toss with cacao tomato sauce
Add roasted vegetables
Top with dark chocolate pieces (they'll melt slightly)
Garnish with fresh basil
Serve immediately
recipe_tips: Cacao adds richness without sweetness
Use chickpea pasta for extra protein
Dark chocolate provides additional flavanols
recipes.recipe_notes: Hearty pasta dish with 26mg flavanols from cacao and dark chocolate

recipes.name: Sweet potato and black bean bowl
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Toss sweet potato cubes with olive oil, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper
Roast for 25-30 minutes until tender and caramelized
Warm black beans in small pot
Arrange spinach in serving bowl
Top with roasted sweet potato and black beans
Add sliced avocado
Squeeze fresh lime juice over the bowl
Season with salt and pepper
Serve warm
recipe_tips: Cut sweet potato into even cubes for uniform cooking
Add jalapeños for heat
Top with cilantro for fresh flavor
recipes.recipe_notes: Hearty plant-based bowl rich in fiber and complex carbohydrates

recipes.name: Grilled salmon with greens and berry dressing
instructions: Season salmon with salt, pepper, and lemon juice
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in pan over medium-high heat
Cook salmon 4-5 minutes per side until flaky
Steam green beans and broccoli until tender-crisp (about 5-7 minutes)
In small bowl, crush blackberries with fork
Mix crushed berries with remaining olive oil and balsamic vinegar
Arrange steamed greens on plate
Top with grilled salmon
Drizzle with blackberry dressing
Serve with dark chocolate squares on the side
recipe_tips: Don't overcook salmon - it should be slightly pink in center
Use tofu instead of salmon for vegan version
Berry dressing adds natural sweetness
recipes.recipe_notes: Omega-3 rich dinner with 40mg flavanols from berries and dark chocolate

recipes.name: Vegetable curry with cocoa
instructions: Heat coconut oil in large pot over medium heat
Sauté diced onion and garlic until fragrant (3-4 minutes)
Add curry powder and cocoa powder, stir for 30 seconds
Add pumpkin cubes and cook for 5 minutes
Pour in coconut milk and add chickpeas
Simmer for 15-20 minutes until pumpkin is tender
Stir in fresh spinach until wilted
Season with salt
Serve over brown rice
recipe_tips: Cocoa adds depth without making curry sweet
Adjust curry powder to taste preference
Add chili for extra heat
recipes.recipe_notes: Warming curry with 65mg flavanols from cocoa powder, perfect for dinner

recipes.name: Olive-Chickpea Waffles
instructions: Preheat waffle iron to medium-high heat
In a bowl, combine chickpea flour, chopped rosemary, baking powder, salt, and black pepper
Stir in the finely diced Kalamata olives until evenly distributed
Whisk in olive oil, then gradually add hot water while stirring until batter is thick but smooth with no lumps
Let batter rest for 2-3 minutes to thicken slightly
Pour batter onto preheated waffle iron (about 120-180ml depending on iron size)
Close lid and cook for 8-12 minutes until golden brown and crispy
Carefully remove waffle and serve immediately topped with hummus
Alternative serving: Top with marinara sauce or serve alongside a fresh salad

recipes.name: Vietnamese Veggie & Rice Noodle Salad
instructions: In a small bowl, combine julienned carrots, daikon radish, cucumber, rice vinegar, sugar, and 1g salt. Mix well and refrigerate for at least 1 hour (or up to 4 days) to pickle

For the avocado-ginger dressing: In a blender, combine avocado, minced ginger, lime juice, lime zest, garlic, and 30ml water. Blend until smooth and creamy like aioli, adding more water if needed. Season with salt and pepper. Transfer to a container and refrigerate until ready to use
Cook the brown rice noodles according to package directions (typically 8-10 minutes in boiling water). Drain and rinse with cool water to stop cooking
Drain the pickled vegetables, reserving some of the pickling liquid if desired for extra tang
In a large bowl, toss the cooked rice noodles with the pickled vegetables
Transfer noodle mixture to a serving bowl
Top with mung bean sprouts and fresh cilantro
Drizzle generously with the avocado-ginger dressing
Garnish with chopped peanuts and optional Sriracha sauce
Serve immediately. Leftover salad ingredients can be refrigerated for up to 3 days; dressing should be consumed within 1 day

recipes.name: Switchel: The Original Sports Drink
instructions: In a glass or shaker bottle, combine all ingredients: water, apple cider vinegar, maple syrup, minced ginger, and sea salt
Shake or stir vigorously to mix well and dissolve the maple syrup and salt
Refrigerate overnight (or at least 5 minutes) to allow flavors to meld and infuse
Strain out the ginger pieces if desired, or leave them in for extra flavor
Serve cold as a refreshing hydration drink
Can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 3 days

recipes.name: Indonesian jackfruit rendang
instructions: Cut the shallots into wedges. Remove the dry outer stems from the lemongrass (reserve them) and cut the soft inner part into large pieces
Peel the ginger and cut into pieces. Add the ginger, garlic, cashew nuts, turmeric, cumin, coriander seeds, cinnamon, cloves, and chili flakes to a food processor and blend into a smooth paste
Place the jackfruit in a sieve, rinse under cold running water and drain well. Roughly slice the jackfruit lengthwise into strips
Heat the peanut oil in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat. Add the curry paste and fry for 5 minutes, stirring constantly to release the aromatics
Add the jackfruit and cook for another 2 minutes, stirring to coat with the paste
Add the coconut milk, water, sweet soy sauce, and the reserved lemongrass stalks. Stir to combine
Bring to a gentle simmer, then cover with a lid and cook for 45 minutes on low heat, stirring occasionally
Remove the lid and let it simmer for another 15 minutes to reduce and thicken the sauce. If needed, use a ladle to break larger pieces of jackfruit into smaller, shredded pieces
Remove the lemongrass stalks and season with salt to taste
Roughly chop the fresh coriander and sprinkle over the rendang just before serving
Serving suggestion: Delicious with jasmine rice, fried onions, and Indonesian atjar (pickled vegetables)

recipes.name: Mediterranean vegetable paella with halloumi
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the risotto rice and fry for 2 minutes while stirring constantly
Add the smoked paprika powder, frozen grilled vegetables, tomato frito, vegetable stock powder, and water to the pan. Stir well to combine

Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low-medium. Cook gently without a lid for 25 minutes, stirring the paella occasionally to prevent sticking. The rice should absorb most of the liquid and become tender
Meanwhile, drain the Kalamata olives and set aside. Finely chop the fresh parsley and set aside
Heat a dry skillet (no oil or butter) over medium-high heat. Cut the halloumi cheese into 2 thick slices
Place the halloumi slices in the hot dry skillet and bake for 3 minutes total, turning halfway through, until golden brown on both sides
Once the rice is cooked and most liquid is absorbed, stir in the drained olives and 2/3 of the chopped parsley. Mix well
Transfer the paella to a serving platter. Season with salt and black pepper to taste
Place the grilled halloumi slices on top of the paella
Garnish with the remaining fresh parsley and serve immediately

recipes.name: Mediterranean vegetable paella
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the risotto rice and fry for 2 minutes while stirring constantly
Add the smoked paprika powder, frozen grilled vegetables, tomato frito, vegetable stock powder, and water to the pan. Stir well to combine
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low-medium. Cook gently without a lid for 20 minutes, stirring the paella occasionally to prevent sticking
Add the cooked chickpeas to the pan and continue cooking for 5 more minutes until the rice is tender and most liquid is absorbed
Meanwhile, drain the Kalamata olives. Finely chop the fresh parsley and set aside
Once the rice is cooked and most liquid is absorbed, stir in the drained olives and 2/3 of the chopped parsley. Mix well
Transfer the paella to a serving platter. Season with salt and black pepper to taste
Garnish with the remaining fresh parsley and serve immediately
Note: This lactose-free version uses chickpeas for added protein instead of halloumi cheese

recipes.name: Leek and feta quiche
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F)
Halve the leeks lengthwise, rinse under cold running water to remove any dirt, and cut into half rings
Cook the leeks in boiling water for 4 minutes until tender. Drain in a colander and drain well, pressing gently to remove excess water
Line a small quiche tin (about 15cm diameter) with the savory pie dough, pressing it into the bottom and up the sides
Crumble the feta cheese into small pieces. Finely chop the fresh dill and parsley
In a bowl, beat the egg and mix with the milk, crumbled feta, chopped parsley, 2/3 of the chopped dill, salt, and black pepper. Stir until well combined
Cut the spring onion into rings
Arrange the cooked leek and spring onion rings on the bottom of the dough-lined quiche tin
Pour the egg and feta mixture evenly over the leeks and onions
Bake the quiche in the preheated oven for about 30 minutes until golden brown and the filling is set
Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack for 15 minutes
Garnish with the remaining fresh dill before serving
Prep tip: You can prepare this quiche 1 day in advance and reheat before serving

recipes.name: Leek quiche (gluten-free, lactose-free)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F)
Halve the leeks lengthwise, rinse under cold running water to remove any dirt, and cut into half rings
Cook the leeks in boiling water for 4 minutes until tender. Drain in a colander and drain well, pressing gently to remove excess water
Line a small quiche tin (about 15cm diameter) with the gluten-free dough, pressing it into the bottom and up the sides. Handle gently as gluten-free dough can be more delicate
Crumble the vegan feta cheese into small pieces. Finely chop the fresh dill and parsley
In a bowl, beat the egg and mix with the plant-based milk, crumbled vegan feta, chopped parsley, 2/3 of the chopped dill, salt, and black pepper. Stir until well combined
Cut the spring onion into rings
Arrange the cooked leek and spring onion rings on the bottom of the dough-lined quiche tin
Pour the egg and vegan feta mixture evenly over the leeks and onions
Bake the quiche in the preheated oven for about 30 minutes until golden brown and the filling is set. Gluten-free dough may brown slightly faster, so check at 25 minutes
Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack for 15 minutes
Garnish with the remaining fresh dill before serving
Note: This version uses gluten-free dough and vegan feta to make it suitable for those with gluten and lactose restrictions
Prep tip: You can prepare this quiche 1 day in advance and reheat before serving

recipes.name: Chocolate chia mousse with probiotics
ingredients: For stewed apples: 120g apples (with skin), 15ml water, 1ml vanilla extract, 1g cinnamon
instructions: Prepare stewed apples: Core apples but do not peel (most of the prebiotic-rich pectin is in the skin). Chop into medium pieces
In a small saucepan, combine chopped apples with water, vanilla extract, and cinnamon
Simmer over medium heat for 10-15 minutes until apples are soft and can be easily mashed
Let stewed apples cool to room temperature
In a high-speed blender, add Greek yogurt, cooled stewed apples, chia seeds, cacao powder, and maple syrup
Blend on high speed until smooth and creamy, scraping down sides as needed
Pour the mixture into a ramekin or small bowl
Cover and chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour. The chia seeds and pectin from the apples will naturally thicken the mousse
Before serving, dust the top with optional extra cacao powder
Note: This mousse is packed with prebiotics (from apple pectin and chia seeds) and probiotics (from Greek yogurt) for gut health

recipes.name: Chocolate chia mousse with prebiotics (lactose-free)
ingredients: For stewed apples: 120g apples (with skin), 15ml water, 1ml vanilla extract, 1g cinnamon
instructions: Prepare stewed apples: Core apples but do not peel (most of the prebiotic-rich pectin is in the skin). Chop into medium pieces
In a small saucepan, combine chopped apples with water, vanilla extract, and cinnamon
Simmer over medium heat for 10-15 minutes until apples are soft and can be easily mashed
Let stewed apples cool to room temperature
In a high-speed blender, add plant-based yogurt, cooled stewed apples, chia seeds, cacao powder, and maple syrup
Blend on high speed until smooth and creamy, scraping down sides as needed
Pour the mixture into a ramekin or small bowl
Cover and chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour. The chia seeds and pectin from the apples will naturally thicken the mousse
Before serving, dust the top with optional extra cacao powder
Note: This lactose-free version uses plant-based yogurt instead of Greek yogurt while maintaining the prebiotic benefits from apple pectin and chia seeds for gut health

recipes.name: Chickpea banana oat breakfast bar
instructions: Note: This recipe makes 1 bar, but is best baked in a batch of 12 bars. Scale up all ingredients by 12x for a full batch
For batch baking: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Line a 23cm square pan with parchment paper, letting some hang over the sides for easy removal. Lightly oil with cooking spray
Drain and rinse the chickpeas over a sieve. Use a potato masher or food processor to mash into tiny pieces
Mash the ripe bananas until smooth, measuring precisely
In a large mixing bowl, combine mashed chickpeas, mashed bananas, peanut butter, oats, chia seeds, pumpkin seeds, chopped almonds, and chocolate chips
Stir ingredients very well, pressing them together to form a sticky, thick batter. The mixture should hold together when pressed
Pour the batter into the prepared pan and press evenly to compact. Sprinkle extra chocolate chips on top if desired
Bake for 30-40 minutes at 180°C until dry, golden on top, and fully set
Let the pan cool for 15 minutes, then lift the parchment paper to release the bars onto a cooling rack
Cool completely for 1 hour before slicing into 12 equal bars
Serve for breakfast with mixed berries, a dollop of yogurt, or eat plain. Store in an airtight container for up to 5 days

recipes.name: Sticky udon noodles with mushrooms and pak choi
instructions: To make the sauce: In a small bowl, whisk together tamari, mirin, maple syrup, chilli oil, cornflour, and lime juice and zest until smooth. Set aside
Cook the udon noodles according to packet instructions. Drain and refresh under cold running water until cool to touch
Warm the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden brown
Add the halved pak choi and cook for 2 minutes until softened but still vibrant green

Pour in the prepared sauce and add the cooked udon noodles. Toss everything together until the noodles and vegetables are coated in a thick, glossy sauce. Add a splash of water if needed to loosen the sauce
Add the edamame beans and sliced spring onions. Cook for 2-3 minutes, tossing gently, until everything is heated through
Serve immediately, garnished with red chilli slices (if using) and a sprinkle of black sesame seeds on top

recipes.name: Sticky rice noodles with mushrooms and pak choi
instructions: To make the sauce: In a small bowl, whisk together tamari, mirin, maple syrup, chilli oil, cornflour, and lime juice and zest until smooth. Set aside
Cook the rice noodles according to packet instructions. Drain and refresh under cold running water until cool to touch
Warm the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden brown
Add the halved pak choi and cook for 2 minutes until softened but still vibrant green

Pour in the prepared sauce and add the cooked rice noodles. Toss everything together until the noodles and vegetables are coated in a thick, glossy sauce. Add a splash of water if needed to loosen the sauce
Add the edamame beans and sliced spring onions. Cook for 2-3 minutes, tossing gently, until everything is heated through
Serve immediately, garnished with red chilli slices (if using) and a sprinkle of black sesame seeds on top

recipes.name: Roasted mushroom and red bean stew
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C fan / 200°C (400°F). Add the roughly chopped mushrooms to a baking tray, drizzle with 7.5ml olive oil, and season generously with salt and pepper. Roast for 20-25 minutes until golden brown
Meanwhile, warm 7.5ml olive oil in a medium saucepan or casserole dish over medium-low heat. Add the chopped garlic and cook for 2-3 minutes until fragrant
Add the diced onion, carrots, celery, and a pinch of salt. Cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are softened and translucent
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, red kidney beans, white miso paste, harissa paste, vegetable stock, and the roasted mushrooms
Bring to a gentle simmer, cover with a lid, and cook for 25-30 minutes until the stew is rich, thick, and glossy
While the stew simmers, cook the pasta according to package instructions in salted boiling water. Drain and set aside
Stir the coconut cream into the stew and season to taste with additional salt and pepper if needed
Serve the stew hot over the cooked pasta with a peppery green salad on the side

recipes.name: Roasted mushroom and red bean stew with gluten-free pasta
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C fan / 200°C (400°F). Add the roughly chopped mushrooms to a baking tray, drizzle with 7.5ml olive oil, and season generously with salt and pepper. Roast for 20-25 minutes until golden brown
Meanwhile, warm 7.5ml olive oil in a medium saucepan or casserole dish over medium-low heat. Add the chopped garlic and cook for 2-3 minutes until fragrant
Add the diced onion, carrots, celery, and a pinch of salt. Cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are softened and translucent
Stir in the chopped tomatoes, red kidney beans, white miso paste, harissa paste, vegetable stock, and the roasted mushrooms
Bring to a gentle simmer, cover with a lid, and cook for 25-30 minutes until the stew is rich, thick, and glossy
While the stew simmers, cook the gluten-free pasta according to package instructions in salted boiling water. Drain and set aside
Stir the coconut cream into the stew and season to taste with additional salt and pepper if needed
Serve the stew hot over the cooked gluten-free pasta with a peppery green salad on the side

recipes.name: Fennel and mushroom risotto
instructions: Warm the vegetable broth in a small saucepan and keep it on low heat
Chop the fennel bulb and slice the mixed mushrooms. Finely chop the onion and mince the garlic
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes
Add the Arborio rice to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes to toast the rice. This enhances the nutty flavor
Add 60ml (1/4 cup) of the warmed broth to the rice and stir constantly until the liquid is almost completely absorbed
Continue adding the broth 60ml at a time, stirring constantly and allowing each addition to be absorbed before adding more
When you have about 60ml of broth left, add the chopped fennel and sliced mushrooms to the risotto
Continue stirring and adding the remaining broth until the rice is cooked al dente and creamy, about 18-20 minutes total
Remove from heat and stir in the grated Parmesan cheese. Season with salt and black pepper to taste
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve immediately while hot and creamy

recipes.name: Pumpkin spice chia pudding
instructions: In a small jar add chia seeds, pumpkin puree, pumpkin pie spices, milk, vanilla, and 30ml maple syrup. Use a fork to stir everything well together, then close the lid of the jar and shake. Cover with a lid or plastic wrap, refrigerate for at least 2 hours (overnight recommended), stirring a few times during the first 15 minutes to prevent lumping. The next day, stir again and serve with chopped pecans coated with remaining 10ml maple syrup. Sprinkle extra pumpkin pie spices on top.

recipes.name: Protein pasta bowl with tofu and dressing
instructions: Cook the gluten-free protein pasta according to the package instructions
Mix the cooked pasta with garlic powder, onion granules, sweet paprika, cumin, cinnamon, garam masala, brown sugar, liquid smoke, ketchup, oil, and 2g salt
Spread the seasoned pasta onto a baking sheet and bake for 15-20 minutes at 180°C (top/bottom heat), turning occasionally
While pasta bakes, dice the smoked tofu and fry until crispy
Toss the crispy tofu with soy sauce, maple syrup, and paprika powder
Make the dressing by mixing plant-based yogurt, frozen dill, frozen parsley, lemon juice, 2g salt, black pepper, pickle juice, and grated garlic
Combine the baked pasta, tofu, mixed vegetables, and dressing in a bowl
Mix well and enjoy
recipe_tips: The baked pasta adds a unique crispy texture
You can prep the tofu and dressing in advance
recipes.recipe_notes: High-protein meal with plant-based yogurt and gluten-free pasta. Great for meal prep.

recipes.name: Eggs with rice crackers and avocado
instructions: Heat 5ml olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat
Cook the Beyond Meat burger patty for 3-4 minutes per side until browned
Remove patty and set aside
Add remaining olive oil to pan
Crack eggs into pan and cook to your preference
Season with salt and pepper
Slice the avocado
Serve eggs with Beyond Meat patty, rice crackers and avocado slices
recipe_tips: Try adding a squeeze of lemon on the avocado
Sprinkle with chili flakes for extra flavor

recipes.name: Pea fritters with herb yoghurt dip
instructions: Cook frozen peas in boiling water for 3 minutes, then drain well
Mash peas roughly with a fork, leaving some texture
Mix in minced garlic, nutritional yeast, chopped parsley and mint
Add chickpea flour, salt, pepper and water, mix until combined
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat
Form mixture into small patties and fry 3-4 minutes per side until golden
For the dip: mix yoghurt with lemon juice, extra chopped mint and parsley, and a pinch of salt
Serve fritters warm with the herb yoghurt dip
recipe_tips: Make extra and refrigerate for quick meals
Add a pinch of cumin for extra flavor

recipes.name: Pea fritters with herb yoghurt dip
instructions: Cook frozen peas in boiling water for 3 minutes, then drain well
Mash peas roughly with a fork, leaving some texture
Mix in minced garlic, nutritional yeast, chopped parsley and mint
Add chickpea flour, salt, pepper and water, mix until combined
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat
Form mixture into small patties and fry 3-4 minutes per side until golden
For the dip: mix yoghurt with lemon juice, extra chopped mint and parsley, and a pinch of salt
Serve fritters warm with the herb yoghurt dip
recipe_tips: Make extra and refrigerate for quick meals
Add a pinch of cumin for extra flavor

recipes.name: Spinach and tomato quiche with puff pastry
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C
Line a small tart tin with puff pastry, prick base with fork
Blind bake pastry for 10 minutes
Sauté diced onion in olive oil until translucent, add minced garlic
Add spinach in batches and let wilt, then drain and squeeze out liquid
Blend tofu, plant-based milk, chickpea flour, mustard, nutritional yeast, turmeric, thyme, salt and pepper until smooth
Mix drained spinach into tofu mixture
Spread filling over pre-baked pastry
Arrange halved cherry tomatoes on top, cut side up
Bake for 30-35 minutes until filling is set and top is golden
Let rest 10 minutes before serving
recipe_tips: Press tofu well for best texture
Can add smoked tofu cubes for extra protein

recipes.name: Spinach and tomato quiche with gluten-free puff pastry
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C
Line a small tart tin with gluten-free puff pastry, prick base with fork
Blind bake pastry for 10 minutes
Sauté diced onion in olive oil until translucent, add minced garlic
Add spinach in batches and let wilt, then drain and squeeze out liquid
Blend tofu, plant-based milk, chickpea flour, mustard, nutritional yeast, turmeric, thyme, salt and pepper until smooth
Mix drained spinach into tofu mixture
Spread filling over pre-baked pastry
Arrange halved cherry tomatoes on top, cut side up
Bake for 30-35 minutes until filling is set and top is golden
Let rest 10 minutes before serving
recipe_tips: Press tofu well for best texture
Can add smoked tofu cubes for extra protein

recipes.name: High-protein chocolate mousse
instructions: Peel the hard-boiled eggs
Add eggs, plant-based milk, cacao powder, maple syrup and vanilla to a blender
Blend until completely smooth and creamy
Transfer to a serving glass or bowl
Refrigerate for 30 minutes for best texture, or serve immediately
recipe_tips: Use cold hard-boiled eggs for best texture
Add more maple syrup if you prefer sweeter
Top with cacao nibs or berries

recipes.name: Peanut butter chocolate bars
instructions: Mix oat flour, 11g peanut butter, 6g maple syrup, 2g coconut oil and a pinch of salt until it forms a soft dough
Press evenly into a small parchment-lined container
Chill for 15-20 minutes
Gently melt remaining peanut butter, coconut oil and maple syrup together until smooth
Pour over the base and spread evenly
Chill again for 10-15 minutes
Melt the dark chocolate with remaining coconut oil
Stir in chopped roasted peanuts
Spread over the peanut butter layer evenly
Refrigerate 1 hour or until firm
Slice into bars and store chilled
recipe_tips: Make a full batch (8x the recipe) in a 20x20cm pan for meal prep
Store in fridge for up to 1 week
Can use almond flour instead of oat flour

recipes.name: Matcha pistachio bliss balls
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine almond flour, pistachio butter, maple syrup, matcha powder and vanilla extract
Stir until the mixture comes together into a soft, dough-like consistency
If too sticky, add more almond flour; if too dry, add more pistachio butter or maple syrup
Roll the mixture into small bite-sized balls using your hands
Lightly dust each ball with extra matcha powder for garnish
Refrigerate for 30 minutes to firm up
recipe_tips: Make a full batch (5x the recipe) for meal prep snacks
Store in fridge for up to 1 week
Can use pistachio matcha crema if available for extra flavor

recipes.name: Smashed potato tacos
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C and line a baking tray
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil, add the potatoes and boil for 20 minutes or until soft
Drain and set aside for 5 minutes
Place the potatoes on the tray ensuring none are touching, and use the base of a glass to press down on each potato, keeping them in one piece
Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper
Bake for 45-50 minutes or until golden and crispy, do not turn over
Mash the avocado and dice the cherry tomatoes
Top each smashed potato with avocado, black beans, cherry tomatoes and crumbled vegan feta
Garnish with fresh coriander and pickled onion
recipe_tips: Use small waxy potatoes for best results
Can substitute regular feta if not vegan
Make extra pickled onions to store in the fridge

recipes.name: Mung bean wraps
instructions: Wash mung beans with water and drain
Soak the mung beans with filtered water in an airtight container and place in the fridge overnight or at least 8 hours
Drain the mung beans and pour into a high-speed blender with the water
Blend on high speed until the batter is a smooth consistency
Heat a medium-sized pan over low-medium heat, spray a bit of oil if not non-stick
Ladle a scoop of batter into the pan and form a circle with the ladle
Cook for 3 minutes before flipping the wrap over
Cook the other side for 1-2 minutes
Remove the wrap and set aside
Use immediately or store in an airtight container
recipe_tips: Make a full batch (8 wraps) for weekly meal prep
Store in airtight container for up to 5 days
Fill with vegetables, hummus, or protein of choice
Soak beans overnight for best results

recipes.name: Soul cacao
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a small pan
Gently warm the cacao, keeping it below 80°C
Whisk until creamy
Pour into your favorite cup and enjoy
recipe_tips: Use high-quality ceremonial cacao for best flavor
Keep temperature below 80°C to preserve nutrients
Can substitute agave for maple syrup

recipes.name: Wieringer bean stew with grilled tofu
instructions: If using dried beans, soak 8-12 hours, drain, rinse, then cook in fresh water for 45-60 minutes until tender
Heat olive oil in a pan and sauté onion and garlic until translucent
Add leek and cook until softened
Add chopped tomatoes, tomato paste, chili flakes and thyme, simmer 1-2 minutes
Add beans and vegetable stock to just cover
Simmer on low heat for 15-20 minutes so flavors combine
Season with salt and pepper
Heat a grill pan with a little oil over medium-high heat
Season tofu with salt and pepper
Fry tofu 3-4 minutes per side until browned and slightly crispy
Spoon bean stew into a deep plate
Place grilled tofu on top
Finish with sumac and a squeeze of lemon juice
recipe_tips: Wieringer beans are a Dutch heirloom variety - use white beans or cannellini as substitute
Press tofu well for crispier results
Can use passata instead of chopped tomatoes

recipes.name: Golden tofu and aubergine curry
instructions: To make the curry paste, add 1 tablespoon of coconut milk, the ginger, coriander stalks, shallots, coriander seeds, turmeric, chilli flakes, black pepper and 1 tablespoon of water to a blender. Blitz until smooth.
Warm the oil in a large frying pan over medium-low heat. Add the curry paste and cook for 10 minutes, stirring often, until fragrant.
Increase the heat slightly, add the aubergine and cook for 5 minutes until just starting to soften, adding a little more oil if needed.
Stir in the remaining coconut milk, tofu, maple syrup, tamari, 200ml water and salt. Simmer for 20 minutes until the aubergine is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Add the sugar snap peas for the final 3-4 minutes of cooking.
Stir in the lime juice, taste and adjust seasoning. Scatter fresh coriander leaves over the curry before serving. Delicious with rice noodles or steamed basmati rice.

recipes.name: Pumpkin-coconut pasta
instructions: Rehydrate the dried soy mince in 100ml hot vegetable stock for 10 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Peel the pumpkin (optional) and cut into small cubes. Finely chop the onion and garlic.
Boil the pumpkin cubes for 10 minutes until soft. Drain, return to the pan, and blend into a smooth purée with a stick blender.
Heat a frying pan with oil and cook the onion for 8 minutes. Add garlic, curry leaves, and rehydrated soy mince. Cook 3 minutes more. Set half of the curry leaves aside on kitchen paper for garnish.
Add coconut milk, pumpkin purée, stock cube, spices, olives, and lemon juice to the pan. Stir and simmer on low heat.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions. Drain and mix with the sauce.
Serve topped with the reserved curry leaves and toasted pine nuts.

recipes.name: Caramelised pear porridge with walnuts
instructions: Cut the pear, remove the core, and slice into thin wedges.
Add the coconut oil and maple syrup to a pan on low heat. Add the pear slices and cook for about 5 minutes. Add the walnuts and cook for a few more minutes until caramelised, stirring occasionally.
Transfer the pear and walnuts to a plate to cool.
Add the oats and plant-based milk to a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Simmer on low heat for about 5 minutes, stirring continuously, until it becomes porridge.
Serve the porridge topped with the caramelised pear, walnuts, and a pinch of cinnamon.

recipes.name: Chocolate cardamom cookies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 160°C (fan/convection).
Put the ground flaxseed in a glass with 45ml water. Stir and let sit 5-10 minutes until thickened.
Crush the cardamom pods in a mortar. Remove the green husks and grind the seeds finely.
In a bowl, mix the almond butter, coconut blossom sugar, and the flax mixture with a spoon.
Add the cocoa powder, oat flour, baking powder, baking soda, 45ml water, ground cardamom, and salt. Knead with your hands until you have a smooth ball of dough.
Divide the dough into 8 pieces and roll into balls.
Place on a baking tray lined with parchment paper and bake 15 minutes. Let cool so they crisp up. Sprinkle with a pinch of coarse sea salt before serving.

recipes.name: Ayurvedic amaranth porridge with roasted figs
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
Bring the plant-based milk and water to a boil, then add the raisins and speculaas spice mix.
Add the amaranth and simmer for about 25 minutes until you have a soft, creamy porridge. Stir well and add extra milk and/or water if needed.
Score the figs in a cross pattern and place them in an oven dish greased with ghee.
Add a little ghee on top of the figs and place the star anise and cinnamon sticks in and around the figs.
Bake the figs for about 15 minutes.
Toast the nuts for 2-3 minutes in a pan with ghee.
Wash an orange and grate a little of the peel (zest).
Fill a bowl with the creamy amaranth, place the figs on top, and finish with the toasted nuts, date syrup, and a little orange zest.

recipes.name: Autumn dal with pumpkin and wholegrain rice
instructions: Cook the wholegrain rice separately according to package instructions.
Rinse the mung dal until the water runs clear.
Heat the ghee in a pan. Add cumin, coriander seeds, and mustard seeds; cook until they start to pop.
Add ginger, turmeric, and asafoetida; cook briefly until fragrant.
Add the rinsed mung dal and stir so it is coated with the spices.
Add the vegetable stock and bring to a boil.
Dice the carrot and pumpkin. Add to the pan. Simmer gently for 25-30 minutes, until the dal is cooked and the vegetables are soft.
Season with salt and add lemon juice.
Stir in spinach at the end to wilt. Top with fresh coriander and serve with the wholegrain rice.

recipes.name: Black coconut-vanilla rice pudding
instructions: Rinse the black rice thoroughly in a sieve.
Cook the black rice in plenty of boiling water for about 40 minutes until done. Drain, then let it sit for 10 minutes with the lid on the pan.
Put 3/4 of the cooked black rice in a blender. Add the coconut milk, vanilla extract, and coconut blossom sugar. Blend until smooth.
Stir the remaining cooked black rice into the blended mixture and finish with coconut flakes.

recipes.name: Turmeric pancakes
instructions: Blend rice milk, apple cider vinegar, buckwheat flour, rice flour, baking powder, salt, turmeric, and cinnamon until smooth
Beat the egg in a separate bowl and stir into the batter
Heat a cast-iron pan with safflower oil over medium heat
Pour about 1 ladle of batter per pancake and fry until bubbles form on top
Flip and cook until golden brown on both sides
Serve with honey and a pinch of cinnamon
recipe_tips: Use a cast-iron pan for best results and even cooking
Don't flip until bubbles appear and edges look set
Add optional dried dandelion as topping for extra spring energy
These pancakes are naturally gluten-free thanks to buckwheat and rice flour
recipes.recipe_notes: Traditional Ayurvedic spring recipe. Turmeric provides anti-inflammatory benefits while buckwheat offers complete protein and magnesium. The warm spices support digestion and the light flours are perfect for spring's kapha-reducing needs.

recipes.name: Sweet Potato and Bean Traybake with Roasted Garlic Yoghurt
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C
Add the sweet potato, mushrooms, red onion, paprika, oregano, 5ml olive oil, 1g salt and black pepper to a baking tray and toss together
Wrap 2 garlic cloves (unpeeled) in foil with 2ml olive oil and tuck into corner of baking tray
Place the tray in the oven and cook for 30 minutes, stirring halfway, until golden
Remove the tray from the oven, then stir in the black beans, butter beans, cherry tomatoes and ginger
Return to the oven for 15-20 minutes, until the tomato skins begin to burst
For the garlic yoghurt: carefully squeeze the flesh of the roasted garlic into a small bowl
Stir the garlic into the coconut yoghurt and add 0.5g salt
Top the vegetables and beans with lime juice, chilli flakes, fresh coriander and 1g sea salt
Serve with the roasted garlic yoghurt on the side

recipes.name: Pumpkin-Spice Chia Pudding with Pear
instructions: Whisk plant-based quark with plant-based yogurt in a bowl
Stir in chia seeds and let rest for 10 minutes
Stir again to prevent clumping
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight
Stir in pumpkin purée and pumpkin-spice mix until well combined
Fold in diced pear
Top with pumpkin seeds, maple syrup drizzle, and sea salt flakes before serving

recipes.name: Coconut curry with vegan dumplings
instructions: Cook rice according to package instructions and set aside
Heat a pan or wok over medium heat and add red curry paste
Mince garlic and grate ginger, add to pan and fry for 1-2 minutes
Add broccoli florets, sliced red pepper, baby corn, and edamame beans
Stir-fry vegetables for 3-4 minutes until slightly tender
Pour in coconut milk and add kaffir lime leaf
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes
Add dumplings and cook for another 3-4 minutes until heated through
Season with salt to taste
Serve over rice, garnish with fresh coriander, sesame seeds, and crispy chili oil
recipe_tips: Can use steam oven method: switch to steam mode after initial 25 min baking, then steam dumplings 5-10 min
Kaffir lime leaf adds authentic Thai flavor but can be omitted
Adjust curry paste amount based on heat preference
Great for meal prep - reheat gently to preserve dumpling texture
recipes.recipe_notes: Aromatic Thai-inspired curry that can be baked in conventional or steam oven. Vegan dumplings make it hearty and satisfying.

recipes.name: Coconut curry with gluten-free dumplings
instructions: Cook rice according to package instructions and set aside
Heat a pan or wok over medium heat and add red curry paste
Mince garlic and grate ginger, add to pan and fry for 1-2 minutes
Add broccoli florets, sliced red pepper, baby corn, and edamame beans
Stir-fry vegetables for 3-4 minutes until slightly tender
Pour in coconut milk and add kaffir lime leaf
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes
Add dumplings and cook for another 3-4 minutes until heated through
Season with salt to taste
Serve over rice, garnish with fresh coriander, sesame seeds, and crispy chili oil

recipes.name: Skyr cheesecake with passionfruit and walnuts
instructions: Toast the walnuts for a few minutes in a dry skillet until golden and fragrant. Let them cool

Blend the dates, walnuts, and oats in a food processor until fine. Press the mixture evenly into a small dish or ramekin lined with baking paper. Smooth the surface with the back of a wet spoon. Chill in the fridge
Place the skyr in a bowl and mix until smooth
In a small pan, stir the gelatin into 15ml cold water. Add 1 tablespoon of the skyr mixture. Bring slowly to a boil while stirring, then simmer gently for 2 minutes
Remove from heat, mix in one quarter of the remaining skyr, then combine with the rest. Pour over the crust and refrigerate to set

In a clean pan, heat 30g of the passionfruit until thawed. Stir in a pinch of gelatin (about 0.4g), bring to a boil, and simmer for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and mix in the remaining 10g of passionfruit
When cooled, pour the passionfruit topping evenly over the cake. Chill for at least 2 hours to set
Serve with fresh red fruit
recipe_tips: Make sure gelatin dissolves completely to avoid lumps
Chill for at least 2 hours for best texture
Use a food processor for smooth walnut-date crust
Passionfruit can be replaced with mango or other tropical fruit
recipes.recipe_notes: Elegant no-bake protein cheesecake made with Icelandic skyr. The walnut-date crust provides healthy fats and natural sweetness, while the passionfruit topping adds tropical flavor.

recipes.name: Lactose-free skyr cheesecake with passionfruit and walnuts
instructions: Toast the walnuts for a few minutes in a dry skillet until golden and fragrant. Let them cool

Blend the dates, walnuts, and oats in a food processor until fine. Press the mixture evenly into a small dish or ramekin lined with baking paper. Smooth the surface with the back of a wet spoon. Chill in the fridge
Place the lactose-free skyr in a bowl and mix until smooth
In a small pan, stir the gelatin into 15ml cold water. Add 1 tablespoon of the skyr mixture. Bring slowly to a boil while stirring, then simmer gently for 2 minutes
Remove from heat, mix in one quarter of the remaining skyr, then combine with the rest. Pour over the crust and refrigerate to set

In a clean pan, heat 30g of the passionfruit until thawed. Stir in a pinch of gelatin (about 0.4g), bring to a boil, and simmer for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and mix in the remaining 10g of passionfruit
When cooled, pour the passionfruit topping evenly over the cake. Chill for at least 2 hours to set
Serve with fresh red fruit
recipe_tips: Lactose-free skyr behaves exactly like regular skyr in this recipe
Make sure gelatin dissolves completely to avoid lumps
Chill for at least 2 hours for best texture
Passionfruit can be replaced with mango or other tropical fruit
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version of elegant no-bake protein cheesecake. Made with lactose-free Icelandic skyr for those with lactose intolerance. Same great taste and protein content as the original.

recipes.name: Lactose-free skyr cheesecake with passionfruit and walnuts
instructions: Toast the walnuts for a few minutes in a dry skillet until golden and fragrant. Let them cool

Blend the dates, walnuts, and oats in a food processor until fine. Press the mixture evenly into a small dish or ramekin lined with baking paper. Smooth the surface with the back of a wet spoon. Chill in the fridge
Place the lactose-free skyr in a bowl and mix until smooth
In a small pan, stir the gelatin into 15ml cold water. Add 1 tablespoon of the skyr mixture. Bring slowly to a boil while stirring, then simmer gently for 2 minutes
Remove from heat, mix in one quarter of the remaining skyr, then combine with the rest. Pour over the crust and refrigerate to set

In a clean pan, heat 30g of the passionfruit until thawed. Stir in a pinch of gelatin (about 0.4g), bring to a boil, and simmer for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and mix in the remaining 10g of passionfruit
When cooled, pour the passionfruit topping evenly over the cake. Chill for at least 2 hours to set
Serve with fresh red fruit
recipe_tips: Lactose-free skyr behaves exactly like regular skyr in this recipe
Make sure gelatin dissolves completely to avoid lumps
Chill for at least 2 hours for best texture
Passionfruit can be replaced with mango or other tropical fruit
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version of elegant no-bake protein cheesecake. Made with lactose-free Icelandic skyr for those with lactose intolerance. Same great taste and protein content as the original.

recipes.name: Creamy tomato gnocchi
instructions: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add the gnocchi and cook for 2-3 minutes until they float. Before draining, scoop out about 60ml of the cooking water and set aside.
In a large non-stick skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped red onion and cook for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the cherry tomatoes to the pan and cook for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they start to burst and release their juices.
Stir in the tomato paste and chili flakes, then season with salt and pepper. Cook for another minute to deepen the flavor.
Pour in the heavy cream and a splash of the reserved gnocchi cooking water. Stir gently and let the sauce simmer for 1-2 minutes until slightly thickened and glossy.
Add the cooked gnocchi straight to the skillet and toss gently to coat in the sauce. If the sauce feels too thick, add a little more pasta water to loosen it up.
Tear the mozzarella into pieces and scatter over the gnocchi. Let it melt slightly into the warm sauce.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh basil leaves and serve immediately.
recipe_tips: Save some gnocchi cooking water - the starchy water helps bind the sauce to the gnocchi
Let the cherry tomatoes burst naturally for the best flavor
Tear the mozzarella by hand for those irresistible cheese pulls
recipes.recipe_notes: Store-bought potato gnocchi works perfectly for this quick one-pan recipe. The combination of burst cherry tomatoes, cream, and melted mozzarella creates a restaurant-quality dish in under 20 minutes.

recipes.name: Pasta e fagioli with walnut pesto
instructions: Warm the olive oil in a large, deep saucepan set over medium heat. Add the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the onion, carrot and a pinch of salt. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened but not coloured.
Add the tomatoes, cannellini beans, miso paste and vegetable stock. Simmer for 20 minutes until slightly thickened and the carrots are tender.
Add the pasta and cavolo nero. Cook for 10-12 minutes until the pasta is al dente.
Meanwhile, make the pesto: blend the parsley, walnuts, garlic, olive oil, chilli flakes and salt in a food processor until smooth.
Serve the pasta e fagioli in a bowl, topped with a spoonful of walnut pesto and a drizzle of olive oil.

recipes.name: Matcha-raspberry chocolate bar
instructions: Line a small tray or plate with baking paper.
If using frozen raspberries, let them sit 5 minutes. Spread raspberries into a tight rectangle about 1-1.5 cm thick. Press gently so berries connect.
Freeze the raspberry layer for 20-30 minutes until firm.
