Alternatively, microwave on high for 1 minute at a time, stopping to stir until oats are tender (3-4 minutes total)
Add grated apple, chopped almonds, raisins, and cinnamon to the pan
Cook until heated through, stirring gently to combine
Stir in protein powder until well mixed
Serve immediately while warm, or place apple, raisins, and almonds on top as garnish and dust with cinnamon
recipe_tips: Pre-cook quinoa in batches for quick breakfast prep throughout the week
Grate apple just before adding to prevent browning
Perfect for winter meal prep - quinoa adds protein and creamy texture
recipes.recipe_notes: Enhanced with protein powder and hemp seeds to boost protein content while maintaining the satisfying winter breakfast appeal

recipes.name: Healthy Chicken Kapsalon
instructions: Cut sweet potatoes into thick fries. Toss with olive oil, smoked paprika, salt and pepper.
Bake fries at 220°C for 25-30 minutes until crispy, flipping halfway through.
Season chicken breast strips with cumin, garlic powder, oregano, salt and pepper. Cook in a pan until golden and cooked through.
Make tzatziki sauce: Scrape out the seeds from 1/4 cucumber and finely grate. Squeeze out excess liquid. Mix with coconut yogurt, minced garlic, and lemon juice. Season with salt.
Shred iceberg lettuce and thinly slice red cabbage. Dice tomato and remaining cucumber. Slice red onion.
Layer fries in a bowl, top with lettuce, red cabbage, tomato, cucumber, and red onion. Add cooked chicken.
Drizzle with homemade tzatziki sauce and sprinkle with pumpkin seeds.
recipe_tips: Soak sweet potato fries in cold water for 30 minutes before roasting for extra crispiness
Don't overcrowd the baking tray - use two trays if needed for better roasting
Adjust sriracha amount in sauce according to spice preference
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Healthy take on Dutch kapsalon using sweet potato fries, lean seasoned chicken, fresh salad, and spicy yogurt sauce

recipes.name: Thai Red Lentil & Chickpea Curry
ingredients: 2 whole bird's eye chillies, finely chopped
4 whole large sweet pointed red peppers, cut into 1cm slices
instructions: Warm olive oil in large saucepan set over medium-low heat
Add garlic, ginger, chillies and curry paste
Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring often, until fragrant
Add red peppers, red lentils, coconut milk, tamari, maple syrup and vegetable stock
Bring to boil then reduce heat and simmer with lid on for 20-25 minutes until lentils are tender
Add chickpeas along with liquid from jar and simmer for 5-10 minutes
Add spinach and cook until just wilted
Stir in lime juice and three-quarters of Thai basil
Taste and adjust seasoning
Serve in bowls, topped with remaining Thai basil, extra chillies, and lime wedges
Delicious with steamed sticky rice or quinoa
recipe_tips: Keep curry mild for sensitive palates or add extra chillies for heat lovers
Curry keeps well in fridge for up to 3 days or freezer for up to 3 months
Let cool completely before storing in airtight containers for best results
recipes.recipe_notes: One-pan curry perfect for batch-cooking - rich, fragrant, and packed with lentils, chickpeas and spinach for fiber, protein and iron

recipes.name: Crispy Aubergine with Spiced Tomato Sauce & Hemp Hearts
instructions: Slice aubergines lengthwise into 1cm thick planks, salt generously and let drain 20 minutes
Meanwhile, make spiced tomato sauce: heat olive oil, sauté chopped shallots until soft
Add minced garlic, cook 1 minute, then add crushed tomatoes, sugar, salt, pepper
Simmer sauce 15 minutes until thickened, stir in fresh basil at end
Pat aubergine slices dry with paper towels
Set up breading station: spelt flour with salt and pepper, oat milk, panko mixed with hemp hearts and nutritional yeast
Dredge aubergine: flour, then milk, then hemp heart panko mixture, pressing gently
Heat olive oil in large pan over medium heat
Fry breaded aubergine slices 3-4 minutes per side until golden and crispy
Season with paprika, oregano, and chili flakes while hot
Serve immediately over warm spiced tomato sauce, garnish with fresh basil
recipe_tips: Spelt flour creates better texture than regular wheat - nuttier and less dense
Hemp hearts in breading boost protein and add omega-3s
Salt aubergine well to remove bitterness and excess moisture
Don't overcrowd pan - fry in batches for maximum crispiness
recipes.recipe_notes: Spelt flour provides superior texture with its lower gluten content and nutty flavor that complements aubergine. Hemp hearts cleverly boost protein while maintaining the crispy coating integrity.

recipes.name: Crispy Herb-Crusted Chicken with Spiced Tomato Sauce
instructions: Cut chicken breast into bite-sized chunks, season with salt and pepper
Make spiced tomato sauce: heat olive oil, sauté chopped shallots until soft
Add minced garlic, cook 1 minute, then add crushed tomatoes, sugar, salt, pepper
Simmer sauce 15 minutes until thickened, stir in fresh basil at end
Set up breading station: seasoned flour, milk, panko with paprika and oregano
Dredge chicken chunks: flour, then milk, then seasoned panko, pressing gently
Heat olive oil in large pan over medium-high heat
Fry breaded chicken pieces 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through
Check internal temperature reaches 75°C
Season with extra paprika and oregano while hot
Serve immediately over warm spiced tomato sauce with fresh basil
recipe_tips: Cut chicken evenly for consistent cooking
Don't overcrowd pan - fry in batches if needed
Press panko coating gently to help it stick
Fresh basil added at end preserves bright flavor
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Crispy herb-crusted chicken with vibrant tomato sauce creates a satisfying comfort food that delivers excellent protein while maintaining indulgent flavors.

recipes.name: Mediterranean Feta & Chickpea Cauliflower Rice Bowl
instructions: If using fresh cauliflower, pulse florets in food processor until rice-sized pieces form
Heat 15ml olive oil in large frying pan over medium heat
Add cauliflower rice and minced garlic, cook for 5-7 minutes until tender
Season cauliflower rice with oregano, salt, pepper, and fresh herbs
Meanwhile, prepare dressing by whisking tahini, lemon juice, remaining olive oil, salt and pepper
Toast pine nuts in dry pan for 2-3 minutes until lightly golden
In large serving bowl, combine warm cauliflower rice with chickpeas
Add bell pepper, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and cucumber
Drizzle with tahini lemon dressing and toss gently to combine
Top with crumbled feta cheese, toasted pine nuts, and hemp hearts
Garnish with fresh herbs before serving

recipes.name: Mediterranean Lactose-Free Feta & Chickpea Cauliflower Rice Bowl
ingredients: Sea salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: If using fresh cauliflower, pulse florets in food processor until rice-sized pieces form
Heat 15ml olive oil in large frying pan over medium heat
Add cauliflower rice and minced garlic, cook for 5-7 minutes until tender
Season cauliflower rice with oregano, salt, pepper, and fresh herbs
Meanwhile, prepare dressing by whisking tahini, lemon juice, remaining olive oil, salt and pepper
Toast pine nuts in dry pan for 2-3 minutes until lightly golden
In large serving bowl, combine warm cauliflower rice with chickpeas
Add bell pepper, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and cucumber
Drizzle with tahini lemon dressing and toss gently to combine
Top with crumbled lactose-free feta cheese, toasted pine nuts, and hemp hearts
Garnish with fresh herbs before serving
recipe_tips: Violife or other quality dairy-free feta provides authentic Mediterranean flavor
Let lactose-free feta come to room temperature before crumbling for best texture
Perfect for those with lactose intolerance who love Mediterranean flavors
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures

recipes.name: Earl Grey Tea-Infused Quinoa Porridge with Caramelized Persimmon
instructions: Prepare Earl Grey tea: Brew 2 strong tea bags in 200ml boiling water for 5 minutes, then remove bags and cool completely
Cook quinoa ahead with tea: Rinse 200g quinoa and simmer in 300ml Earl Grey tea + 100ml water for 15 minutes until tender. Store in fridge for up to 5 days
In medium saucepan, combine oats, 80g tea-infused pre-cooked quinoa, oat milk, remaining Earl Grey tea, and pinch of salt
Bring to gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally
Meanwhile, heat small frying pan over medium heat
Add diced persimmon with half the maple syrup and cinnamon
Cook persimmon gently for 6-8 minutes until caramelized and jammy
When porridge is creamy and Earl Grey-scented, remove from heat
Stir in protein powder, vanilla extract, ginger, and cardamom until smooth
Add hemp hearts and remaining maple syrup, mix well
Serve in warm bowl topped with caramelized persimmon
Sprinkle with chopped walnuts, pumpkin seeds, and extra cinnamon
recipe_tips: Earl Grey's bergamot adds elegant citrus notes that pair beautifully with persimmon
If persimmons aren't available, use very ripe pears for similar sweetness
Tea-infused quinoa can be batch-cooked and adds sophisticated flavor to the porridge
recipes.recipe_notes: Elegant breakfast porridge infused with Earl Grey tea's bergamot essence, topped with caramelized persimmon for natural sweetness and winter warmth

recipes.name: Chocolate Overnight Oats with Dark Chocolate Crumble
instructions: In a jar or container, mix together oats, cocoa protein powder, cocoa powder, flax seeds, and chia seeds
Add soy milk, sweetener, and vanilla extract, stir well to combine all dry ingredients
Pour the mixture into your storage jar and refrigerate overnight or at least 1 hour
In separate small bowl, mix vanilla yogurt - this will be your middle layer
Melt dark chocolate with coconut oil in microwave or double boiler until smooth
Crush rice cake into small pieces and mix with half the melted chocolate
When ready to serve, layer the overnight oats base in serving jar
Add yogurt layer on top of the oat base
Top with chocolate rice cake crumble and drizzle remaining melted chocolate
Refrigerate for 1 hour or overnight before enjoying for best texture
recipe_tips: Prepare multiple jars at once for easy grab-and-go breakfasts
Cocoa protein powder provides rich chocolate flavor while boosting protein content
Rice cake adds satisfying crunch - can substitute with granola if preferred
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Indulgent chocolate overnight oats with impressive protein content, perfect for meal prep and satisfying chocolate cravings healthily

recipes.name: Caramelized Fig & Pistachio Breakfast Bowl
instructions: Begin by slicing the figs in half (or quarters if you prefer larger pieces)
Heat a medium frying pan over medium-low heat
Melt the butter and cinnamon in the pan, stirring to combine
Drizzle the honey into the butter mixture and stir with a wooden spoon
Gently place the fig halves cut-side down in the pan
Leave for 3-4 minutes to slowly caramelize without stirring
Once the figs are nicely soft and jammy, remove from pan and set aside on a plate
Using the same pan with the remaining juices, add the oats and pistachios
Stir continuously and cook for 2-3 minutes until the oats have toasted and browned nicely
Place the Greek yogurt in a serving bowl
Top the yogurt with the toasted oat and pistachio mixture
Arrange the caramelized figs on top of the granola mix
Drizzle with extra honey if desired and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Choose figs that are ripe but still firm - they'll caramelize better without falling apart
Don't move the figs while caramelizing to get a beautiful golden color
Save the pan juices to toast the oats - they add incredible flavor
This recipe works beautifully with any type of yogurt you prefer
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. A stunning autumn breakfast that celebrates fresh figs at their peak, with warm spices and crunchy pistachios creating the perfect seasonal start to your day

recipes.name: Power Oats
instructions: In a small saucepan, bring water to a boil with a pinch of salt
Add rolled oats and reduce heat to medium-low
Simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally until creamy
Stir in chia seeds, hemp hearts, and ground flax seeds
Cook for another 2 minutes until seeds are well incorporated
Remove from heat and stir in cinnamon
Transfer to serving bowl
Top with almond butter, sliced banana, and mixed berries
Sprinkle with pumpkin seeds and sunflower seeds
Serve hot

recipes.name: Superfood Breakfast Bowl
instructions: Add frozen bananas, açai puree, and mixed frozen berries to a high-speed blender
Add coconut milk and 1 tbsp almond butter
Blend until thick and smooth, like soft-serve ice cream consistency
You may need to stop and scrape down sides several times
The mixture should be thick enough to eat with a spoon
Transfer to a chilled bowl
Top with granola, sliced kiwi, and goji berries
Drizzle with remaining almond butter
Sprinkle with coconut flakes, chia seeds, and mint leaves
Serve immediately
recipe_tips: Use frozen fruit for the best thick, creamy texture
Don't add too much liquid - you want a thick smoothie base
Arrange toppings in rows for an Instagram-worthy presentation
Eat immediately as the base will start to melt
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Vibrant superfood bowl packed with antioxidants and natural energy - like a healthy ice cream sundae for breakfast

recipes.name: Strawberry Protein Smoothie
instructions: Add cashew milk, frozen banana, and frozen strawberries to blender
Add rolled oats, chia seeds, and hemp hearts
Pour in fresh lime juice and vanilla extract
Add protein powder and almond butter
Add maple syrup if extra sweetness is desired
Blend on high speed for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth
Stop and scrape down sides if needed
Blend again until creamy and no chunks remain
Pour into large glass and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Use frozen fruit for the best thick, creamy texture
Let oats soak in the liquid for 2-3 minutes before blending for smoother texture
Adjust sweetness with more banana or maple syrup as needed
Add ice cubes for an even thicker consistency
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Protein-packed smoothie perfect for post-workout recovery or energizing breakfast with triple superfood seeds

recipes.name: Matcha Cloud Smoothie
instructions: Add coconut milk and Greek yogurt to blender first
Add fresh spinach leaves, packing them down
Add frozen banana and ice cubes
Sift matcha powder to remove any lumps, then add to blender
Add protein powder, almond butter, honey, and vanilla extract
Blend on low speed first to break down spinach
Increase to high speed and blend for 90 seconds until completely smooth
The color should be a beautiful pale green
Pour into glass and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Use ceremonial grade matcha for the best flavor and color
Sift matcha powder to prevent lumps in your smoothie
Start blending on low to properly break down the spinach
Frozen banana gives the smoothie a creamy, cloud-like texture
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Elegant green smoothie with subtle matcha flavor and hidden vegetables - like drinking a nutritious cloud

recipes.name: Very Berry Smoothie
instructions: Add almond milk to blender first
Add frozen banana, strawberries, and blueberries
Add almond butter, chia seeds, and hemp hearts
Pour in vanilla extract and maple syrup
Add ice cubes for extra thickness
Blend on high speed for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth
The smoothie should be thick and creamy with a beautiful purple color
Taste and adjust sweetness with more maple syrup if needed
Pour into glass and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Use a mix of frozen berries for the best texture and flavor
Let chia seeds sit in the liquid for a minute before blending for smoother texture
Add a handful of ice for an extra thick, milkshake-like consistency
Garnish with fresh berries and a sprinkle of chia seeds
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Classic berry smoothie with a protein boost - naturally sweet and packed with antioxidants for a healthy start to your day

recipes.name: Super Simple Quinoa Chilli
instructions: Place a large, heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat and add a drizzle of olive oil
Once hot, add the red onion, garlic and a sprinkle of salt and pepper
Cook for 4-5 minutes, until softened and translucent
Add the paprika, cumin and tomato purée, stirring until everything is coated in the spices and tomato
After about a minute, the mixture should smell lovely and fragrant
Add in the quinoa, drained chickpeas and kidney beans, and the tin of tomatoes
Fill the empty tin with water and add this liquid to the pan
Cover the pan and leave to simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent the quinoa from sticking
Once the quinoa is tender, stir in the almond butter, lime juice, miso and maple syrup
Adjust seasoning to taste, and serve with extra lime squeezed over
Top with chopped avocado and fresh coriander
recipe_tips: Stir occasionally during simmering to prevent quinoa from sticking to the bottom

Plant-based milks may have different consistencies

recipes.name: Pre-Workout Protein Smoothie
instructions: Add all ingredients to a high-speed blender
Blend on high for 60-90 seconds until completely smooth
Add more almond milk if needed for desired consistency
Pour into a large glass and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Use frozen berries for a thicker smoothie texture
Add protein powder last to prevent clumping

instructions: Mix oat flour, protein powder, cinnamon, baking powder and salt in a bowl

Roll dough into 8 small balls, then roll into finger shapes
Heat coconut oil in non-stick pan over medium heat
Cook churro bites 2-3 minutes per side until golden
Dust with extra cinnamon while warm
recipe_tips: Don't overmix the dough to keep texture light
Make sure pan isn't too hot to prevent burning
Store leftovers in airtight container for 2 days
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Satisfies sweet cravings while supporting blood sugar stability and providing protein for muscle recovery

recipes.name: Protein Cashew Nut Mousse
instructions: Drain and rinse soaked cashews thoroughly
Add cashews, protein powder, cocoa powder to high-speed blender
Pour in plant milk, maple syrup, vanilla and salt
Blend on high 2-3 minutes until completely smooth and creamy
Taste and adjust sweetness if needed
Pour into serving glasses and chill 1 hour
Top with dark chocolate chips before serving
recipe_tips: Soak cashews in hot water for quicker softening (30 minutes)
Use less plant milk for thicker mousse, more for lighter texture
Can be made 2 days ahead and stored covered in fridge
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures

Blend on high 90 seconds until smooth and creamy

Frozen banana makes smoothie thicker and creamier

recipes.name: Spirulina protein smoothie bowl

recipes.name: Aggie's Matcha Latte
instructions: Sift matcha powder into a small bowl to remove lumps
Add 60ml hot (not boiling) plant milk to matcha
Whisk vigorously until completely smooth paste forms
Heat remaining plant milk in saucepan until steaming
Whisk in honey, vanilla and salt until dissolved
Pour hot milk into matcha paste, whisking constantly
Serve immediately in large mug
recipe_tips: Use ceremonial grade matcha for best flavor
Don't use boiling water - it makes matcha bitter
Whisk thoroughly to prevent lumps
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures

recipes.name: Breakfast Muffins
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C fan/375°F
Grease 6-cup muffin tin or line with cases
Beat eggs in large bowl, whisk in milk and olive oil
Add flour, salt and pepper, mix until just combined
Fold in grated cheese and chopped vegetables
Divide mixture between muffin cases
Bake 18-20 minutes until golden and set

Drain spinach well to prevent soggy muffins

Plant-based milks may have different consistencies

instructions: Mix eggs, yogurt, flour and 1 tbsp olive oil into smooth dough
Knead lightly, rest 10 minutes
Roll out to 25cm circle on floured surface
Heat remaining oil in large oven-safe pan
Cook flatbread 3-4 minutes until golden underneath
Top with halved tomatoes, garlic, herbs and cheese
Transfer pan to 200°C oven for 8-10 minutes
Finish with fresh herbs and black pepper
recipe_tips: Don't roll dough too thin or it will break
Use cast iron pan for best results
Serve immediately while cheese is melted
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures

instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C fan/355°F
Brown ground chicken in olive oil until cooked through
Beat eggs with cream, half the cheddar and seasonings
Layer chicken and vegetables in greased baking dish
Pour egg mixture over, top with remaining cheese
Sprinkle with parmesan and fresh herbs
Bake 25-30 minutes until set and golden
Rest 5 minutes before serving
recipe_tips: Drain excess liquid from vegetables before layering
Don't overbake or eggs will become rubbery
Can be made ahead and reheated
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies

recipes.name: Protein Pancakes
instructions: Blend oats in blender until flour-like consistency
Mash banana in bowl until smooth
Beat eggs, add mashed banana, protein powder, milk and vanilla
Mix in oat flour, baking powder, cinnamon and pinch salt
Let batter rest 5 minutes to thicken
Heat coconut oil in non-stick pan over medium heat
Pour 1/3 cup batter per pancake
Cook 2-3 minutes until bubbles form, flip and cook 2 minutes more
recipe_tips: Don't flip too early or pancakes will break
Keep cooked pancakes warm in low oven
Serve with fresh berries and maple syrup
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Energy-boosting pancakes that satisfy cravings while regulating digestion and blood sugar during menstruation

recipes.name: Protein Truffle Bites
instructions: Mix protein powder, oats, cocoa powder, chia seeds and salt in bowl
In separate bowl, mix almond butter, maple syrup and melted coconut oil
Combine wet and dry ingredients until mixture holds together
Add water 1 tsp at a time if too dry
Chill mixture 15 minutes until firm
Roll into 8 small balls with damp hands
Roll each ball in cocoa powder to coat
Store in fridge until ready to eat
recipe_tips: Chill mixture if too soft to roll
Use damp hands to prevent sticking
Store in airtight container for up to 1 week
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Curbs sugar cravings while providing protein and magnesium for muscle relaxation during menstruation

recipes.name: Peanut Butter Cups
instructions: Line 6 mini muffin cases in tin
Melt 40g chocolate with 1 tbsp coconut oil gently
Spoon thin layer into each case, chill 10 minutes
Mix peanut butter with maple syrup and salt
Spoon peanut mixture over chocolate layer
Melt remaining chocolate with remaining coconut oil

recipe_tips: Don't overheat chocolate or it will seize
Use natural peanut butter without added sugar

recipes.name: Energy Bites
instructions: Soak dates in warm water for 10 minutes if very dry
Add dates to food processor, pulse until paste forms
Add oats, pulse until roughly chopped
Add almond butter, chia seeds, hemp hearts, cinnamon and salt
Process until mixture holds together when pressed
Roll into 12 small balls with damp hands

Store in fridge up to 1 week
recipe_tips: If mixture too wet, add more oats
If too dry, add 1 tsp water or more nut butter
Roll in coconut or cocoa powder for variety
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Natural iron and fiber providing gentle, quick energy that's easy on digestion during menstruation

recipes.name: Salmon Cucumber Bites
instructions: Slice cucumber into 1cm thick rounds
Mix cream cheese with chopped dill, lemon juice and pepper
Using small spoon, top each cucumber slice with cream cheese
Arrange smoked salmon pieces on top
Garnish with capers and extra dill
Chill until ready to serve
Serve within 2 hours for best texture
recipe_tips: Choose firm cucumber for best bite
Pat salmon dry before arranging
Make just before serving to prevent soggy cucumber
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures

recipes.name: Garlic Butter Chicken
instructions: Season chicken breast with salt and pepper
Heat 1 tbsp butter in pan over medium-high heat
Cook chicken 6-7 minutes per side until golden and cooked through
Remove chicken, rest under foil
Add remaining butter and minced garlic to pan
Cook 1 minute until fragrant

Simmer 2 minutes, pour over sliced chicken

Use meat thermometer - internal temp should reach 75°C

recipes.name: Protein Mug Cake
instructions: Mix protein powder, cocoa powder, baking powder and salt in microwave-safe mug
In small bowl, whisk egg, almond milk, almond butter and vanilla
Pour wet ingredients into mug with dry ingredients
Stir well until smooth batter forms

recipes.name: Filled Portobello Mushrooms with Protein-Enhanced Pesto
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C
For the protein-enhanced pesto: blend basil, arugula, pistachios, Parmesan, and silken tofu in a food processor

Place stuffed mushrooms in an oven dish

recipe_tips: Use well-drained silken tofu for the creamiest pesto texture
Cooked lentils can be from a can (drained and rinsed) or pre-cooked

Serve with extra pesto drizzled on top for maximum flavor
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures

instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Place cherry tomatoes in an oven dish with olive oil, salt, and pepper

Cook pasta according to package instructions until al dente
While pasta cooks, blend ripe avocado with cooking cream, salt, and pepper until smooth

Serve topped with roasted cherry tomatoes and torn fresh mozzarella
Season with additional salt and pepper if needed

Roast tomatoes until they start to burst for maximum flavor
Mix avocado sauce with hot pasta immediately to prevent browning
Add a squeeze of lemon juice to avocado sauce to prevent oxidation
Fresh basil leaves make an excellent garnish
Plant-based milks may have different consistencies
Coconut cream works best for rich, creamy textures
recipes.recipe_notes: Lactose-free version using plant-based dairy alternatives. Creamy, nutritious pasta featuring healthy avocado instead of heavy cream-based sauces. The roasted tomatoes add sweetness and depth to this satisfying meal.

recipes.name: Gluten-Free Lentil Pasta with Protein-Enhanced Avocado Sauce
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Place cherry tomatoes in an oven dish with olive oil, salt, and pepper
Roast tomatoes for 15-20 minutes until soft and slightly caramelized
Cook lentil pasta according to package instructions (usually 8-10 minutes)
While pasta cooks, blend ripe avocado with lactose-free cream, salt, and pepper until smooth
Drain pasta and mix immediately with the creamy avocado sauce
Fold in cooked edamame beans for extra protein and texture
Serve topped with roasted cherry tomatoes and lactose-free mozzarella

This version provides exceptional protein content for plant-based eaters

recipes.name: High-protein pistachio cheesecake
instructions: Preheat oven to 163°C (325°F)
Add all ingredients (cottage cheese, Greek yogurt, egg, pistachios, maple syrup, almond flour, and vanilla) to a blender
Blend on high speed until completely smooth with no pistachio chunks remaining
Pour the mixture into a small ramekin or individual baking dish (about 10cm/4 inches) lined with parchment paper
Bake for 15-20 minutes. The edges should feel set but the center should still have a slight jiggle when you move the dish
Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature
Transfer to the fridge and chill for at least 2-4 hours (overnight is best)
Serve chilled, optionally topped with extra crushed pistachios
recipe_tips: The center will set completely as it cools in the fridge - don't overbake
For a sweeter cheesecake, add an extra tablespoon of maple syrup

recipes.name: Keto plant-based meat and broccoli stir-fry
instructions: Heat coconut oil in large wok over high heat
Stir-fry beef slices 2-3 minutes until browned
Remove beef, set aside
Add broccoli to wok, stir-fry 3-4 minutes until crisp-tender
Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds
Return beef to wok
Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, erythritol, and vinegar
Pour sauce over beef and broccoli, toss to coat
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds
recipe_tips: Keep heat high for proper stir-fry texture
Don't overcook broccoli - it should stay bright green
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Classic takeout flavors made keto-friendly and gluten-free

recipes.name: Keto stuffed bell peppers
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C
Blanch bell peppers in boiling water 3 minutes, drain
Heat olive oil, sauté onion and garlic until soft
Add ground turkey, cook until browned
Stir in cauliflower rice, tomato paste, paprika, cumin
Cook 5 minutes, season with salt and pepper
Stuff peppers with turkey mixture
Top with cheddar cheese
Bake 25-30 minutes until peppers are tender
Garnish with fresh parsley
recipe_tips: Choose peppers that stand upright for easier stuffing
Make cauliflower rice by pulsing florets in food processor
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Comfort food made keto with cauliflower rice instead of grains

recipes.name: Lentil and plant-based protein meatball power bowl
instructions: Season turkey with cumin, paprika, salt, pepper
Form into small meatballs, brown in olive oil
Cook meatballs through, about 10 minutes
Whisk tahini, lemon juice, water to make dressing
Layer spinach in bowl
Top with warm lentils and sweet potato
Add meatballs and crumbled feta
Drizzle with tahini dressing
Garnish with fresh herbs
recipe_tips: Don't overwork meatballs to keep them tender
Roast sweet potato at 400°F for 25 minutes
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Mediterranean-inspired power lunch with complete proteins

recipes.name: Lean plant-based meat and black bean power chili
instructions: Heat olive oil in large pot over medium-high heat
Brown ground beef, breaking into small pieces
Add onion and bell pepper, cook 5 minutes
Add garlic, cook 1 minute
Stir in tomato paste, spices, cook 1 minute
Add tomatoes, black beans, broth
Simmer 20 minutes until thickened
Serve topped with Greek yogurt and cilantro
recipe_tips: Brown meat well for deeper flavor
Chili tastes better the next day
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Hearty, protein-packed chili perfect for meal prep

recipes.name: Balanced beetroot broccoli bowl with grilled plant-based protein
ingredients: 120g grilled kipstukjes van vegetarische slager breast
instructions: Cook the beetroot until tender (boil or roast, about 30 min), then peel
Make the hummus: blend beetroot, chickpeas, tahini, cumin, garlic, lemon juice, and chickpea water. Add the ice cube while blending. Season with salt and pepper until smooth
Heat 15ml olive oil in a pan and sear the broccoli steak for 2-3 minutes per side until browned and tender
Grill chicken breast seasoned with cumin, salt, and pepper until cooked through (165°F internal temp)
Make the vinaigrette: whisk together extra virgin olive oil, balsamic vinegar, chilli, parsley, and agave
Spread hummus on the plate, top with broccoli and chicken, drizzle with vinaigrette
Serve with rocket-cucumber-mint salad with lemon juice and gluten-free sourdough or quinoa
recipe_tips: Make hummus ahead and store in fridge for up to 5 days
The ice cube makes the hummus extra creamy
Meal prep friendly - assemble fresh for best texture
Use pre-cooked chicken for faster assembly
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Vibrant beetroot hummus bowl with lean grilled chicken and crunchy broccoli steaks. High protein version with the same balanced flavors.

recipes.name: Venezuelan arepa with shredded plant-based protein (reina pepiada)
ingredients: 120g cooked shredded kipstukjes van vegetarische slager breast
instructions: Make arepa dough: mix masarepa, warm water, salt, and olive oil until smooth. Let rest 5 minutes
Divide dough into 2 equal portions and form into thick discs (about 1.5cm thick, 10cm diameter)
Heat a lightly oiled griddle or pan over medium heat
Cook arepas 5-7 minutes per side until golden crust forms and they sound hollow when tapped
While arepas cook, make filling: mix shredded chicken with mashed avocado, mayonnaise, lime juice, cumin, cilantro, salt and pepper
When arepas are done, let cool 2 minutes, then slice open like a pita pocket
Stuff generously with chicken-avocado mixture (reina pepiada)
Serve warm
recipe_tips: Arepas should have a crispy outside and soft inside
Don't make them too thin or they'll be dry
Can finish in oven at 180°C for 10 minutes for extra crispiness
Reina pepiada is the classic Venezuelan chicken-avocado filling
Use rotisserie chicken for faster prep
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Classic Venezuelan arepa with homemade corn cakes and the famous 'reina pepiada' chicken-avocado filling. Naturally gluten-free and packed with protein.

recipes.name: Traditional plant-based meat Rendang with Coconut Rice & Toasted Peanuts
ingredients: 600g Beyond Meat gehakt chuck or shin, cubed
instructions: Soak dried chilies in warm water for 15 minutes, remove seeds
Make spice paste: blend soaked chilies, shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal, lemongrass, and candlenuts until smooth
Cook jasmine rice with 200ml coconut milk and water for fragrant coconut rice
Heat peanut oil in heavy-bottomed pot, fry spice paste 8-10 minutes until fragrant and dark
Add beef cubes, brown on all sides for 5 minutes
Pour in coconut milk and coconut cream, add tamarind paste, palm sugar
Add ground spices (coriander, cumin, fennel) and torn kaffir lime leaves
Bring to boil, then reduce to low simmer
Cook uncovered 1.5-2 hours, stirring occasionally, until sauce reduces and darkens
In final 30 minutes, sauce should be thick and coating the beef
Season with salt, adjust sweetness and sourness with palm sugar and tamarind
Serve over coconut rice, garnish with crushed toasted peanuts
recipe_tips: Authentic rendang should be dry with dark, caramelized sauce
Don't rush - slow cooking develops the complex flavors
Candlenuts are traditional but macadamias work well
Toast peanuts separately to maintain crunch
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Traditional Indonesian rendang requires patience but delivers incredible depth of flavor. The long, slow cooking transforms tough beef into tender, spice-coated perfection.

recipes.name: Crispy Herb-Crusted plant-based protein with Spiced Tomato Sauce
ingredients: 500g kipstukjes van vegetarische slager breast, cut into chunks
instructions: Cut chicken breast into bite-sized chunks, season with salt and pepper
Make spiced tomato sauce: heat olive oil, sauté chopped shallots until soft
Add minced garlic, cook 1 minute, then add crushed tomatoes, sugar, salt, pepper
Simmer sauce 15 minutes until thickened, stir in fresh basil at end
Set up breading station: seasoned flour, milk, panko with paprika and oregano
Dredge chicken chunks: flour, then milk, then seasoned panko, pressing gently
Heat olive oil in large pan over medium-high heat
Fry breaded chicken pieces 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through
Check internal temperature reaches 75°C
Season with extra paprika and oregano while hot
Serve immediately over warm spiced tomato sauce with fresh basil
recipe_tips: Cut chicken evenly for consistent cooking
Don't overcrowd pan - fry in batches if needed
Press panko coating gently to help it stick
Fresh basil added at end preserves bright flavor
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Crispy herb-crusted chicken with vibrant tomato sauce creates a satisfying comfort food that delivers excellent protein while maintaining indulgent flavors.

recipes.name: Mediterranean plant-based Cauliflower Rice Bowl
instructions: If using fresh cauliflower, pulse florets in food processor until rice-sized pieces form
Season diced chicken with oregano, salt, pepper, and half the minced garlic
Heat 15ml olive oil in large frying pan over medium-high heat
Cook seasoned chicken for 6-8 minutes until golden brown and cooked through, set aside
In same pan, add cauliflower rice and remaining garlic, cook for 5-7 minutes until tender
Season cauliflower rice with salt, pepper, and fresh herbs
Meanwhile, prepare dressing by whisking tahini, lemon juice, remaining olive oil, salt and pepper
In large serving bowl, combine warm cauliflower rice and cooked chicken
Add chickpeas, bell pepper, cherry tomatoes, red onion, and cucumber
Drizzle with tahini lemon dressing and toss gently to combine
Sprinkle with hemp hearts and fresh herbs before serving

recipes.name: Hearty plant-based meat & Vegetable Stew
ingredients: 150g lean Beyond Meat gehakt stewing meat, cubed
instructions: Heat olive oil in heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat
Season beef cubes with salt and pepper
Brown beef on all sides (5-6 minutes total)
Add diced onion and cook until softened (5 minutes)
Add garlic and cook 1 minute until fragrant
Add chopped carrots, celery, and potato
Pour in beef stock and canned tomatoes
Add thyme and bay leaves
Bring to boil, then reduce heat to low
Cover and simmer 60-75 minutes until beef is tender
Remove bay leaves before serving
Adjust seasoning to taste
recipe_tips: Brown the beef well for deeper flavor
Don't rush the simmering - tender beef takes time
Make extra for leftovers - tastes even better next day
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Rich in heme iron for optimal absorption, perfect for replenishing iron during menstruation

recipes.name: BEA Salad (plant-based bacon, Egg, Avocado)
instructions: Bring water to boil in small saucepan
Gently lower eggs into boiling water
Cook for 6-7 minutes for soft-boiled, 8-9 for medium
Transfer eggs to cold water to stop cooking
Cook bacon in dry pan until crispy (4-5 minutes)
Arrange mixed greens in serving bowl
Add sliced cucumber and halved cherry tomatoes
Peel and slice avocado, arrange on top
Carefully peel soft-boiled eggs and halve
Place egg halves on salad
Sprinkle with crispy bacon pieces
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice, season to taste
recipe_tips: Use very fresh eggs for perfect soft-boiled texture
Don't overdress - let the ingredients shine
Serve immediately while eggs are warm
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Protein and healthy fat powerhouse with choline for hormone support during menstruation

recipes.name: Coconut Meatballs
instructions: Mix ground beef, coconut, egg, minced garlic and herbs in bowl
Add cumin, coriander, salt and pepper
Mix gently with hands until just combined
Roll into 10 equal meatballs
Heat coconut oil in large pan over medium heat
Cook meatballs 12-15 minutes, turning every 3-4 minutes
Cook until golden brown and cooked through
Serve with steamed vegetables or rice
recipe_tips: Don't overmix meat or meatballs will be tough
Make sure oil is hot before adding meatballs
Test one meatball first to check seasoning
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Iron-rich meatballs with healthy coconut fats for long-lasting energy and reduced cravings during menstruation

recipes.name: plant-based protein Crust Casserole
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C fan/355°F
Brown ground chicken in olive oil until cooked through
Beat eggs with cream, half the cheddar and seasonings
Layer chicken and vegetables in greased baking dish
Pour egg mixture over, top with remaining cheese
Sprinkle with parmesan and fresh herbs
Bake 25-30 minutes until set and golden
Rest 5 minutes before serving
recipe_tips: Drain excess liquid from vegetables before layering
Don't overbake or eggs will become rubbery
Can be made ahead and reheated
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. High protein casserole supporting muscle recovery and stable blood sugar, with calcium for hormone health during menstruation

recipes.name: Egg & plant-based meat Protein Bowl
instructions: Heat olive oil in large pan over medium-high heat
Add ground beef, cook 5-6 minutes breaking up with spoon
Add minced garlic, paprika, cumin, salt and pepper
Cook 2 minutes until beef is browned and cooked through
Push beef to one side, add spinach to pan
Cook until wilted, about 2 minutes
Crack eggs into pan, scramble with beef and spinach
Serve in bowl topped with sliced ripe avocado
recipe_tips: Don't overcook eggs - they continue cooking in hot pan
Use grass-fed beef for better nutrition
Add ripe avocado just before serving
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Powerhouse of heme iron and complete protein to restore energy and support hormone health during menstruation

recipes.name: Garlic Butter plant-based protein
instructions: Season chicken breast with salt and pepper
Heat 1 tbsp butter in pan over medium-high heat
Cook chicken 6-7 minutes per side until golden and cooked through
Remove chicken, rest under foil
Add remaining butter and minced garlic to pan
Cook 1 minute until fragrant
Add wine, thyme, rosemary and lemon juice
Simmer 2 minutes, pour over sliced chicken
recipe_tips: Don't move chicken too early or it won't brown
Use meat thermometer - internal temp should reach 75°C
Rest chicken 5 minutes before slicing
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. High-protein chicken supporting energy levels and hormone balance with zinc and B vitamins during menstruation

recipes.name: Burgers with Sweet Potato Fries and Grilled Vegetables
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Cut sweet potatoes into wedges, toss with olive oil, salt, and pepper
Roast sweet potato wedges for about 25 minutes until golden and tender
If using fresh mince: mix beef with garlic, beaten egg, and breadcrumbs, then shape into patty
If using pre-made burger: season lightly with garlic and cook directly
Grill or pan-fry the burger patty until cooked through (internal temp 70°C)
Meanwhile, grill eggplant and zucchini slices until tender and slightly charred
Toast or warm the burger bun
Assemble burger with patty, grilled vegetables, bean sprouts, and fresh salad
Serve with roasted sweet potato fries on the side
recipe_tips: Don't press down on burger while cooking - it releases juices
Let burger rest for 2-3 minutes after cooking for better texture
Sweet potato wedges get crispier if not overcrowded on the tray
Grill vegetables until they have nice char marks for extra flavor
Toast bun lightly for better texture and to prevent sogginess
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Classic comfort food elevated with sweet potato fries and grilled vegetables. The combination provides complete nutrition while satisfying burger cravings.

recipes.name: High-Protein Power Bowl with Lean plant-based meat & Sweet Potato
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and line a baking tray
Cube sweet potato and toss with half the olive oil, salt, and paprika
Roast sweet potato for 20-25 minutes until tender and lightly caramelized
Season beef cubes with garlic powder, black pepper, and remaining salt
Heat remaining olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat
Cook beef cubes for 6-8 minutes, turning to brown all sides
Let beef rest for 2 minutes to retain juices
Slice avocado and arrange in serving bowl
Add cottage cheese to one section of the bowl
Place roasted sweet potato and cooked beef in separate sections
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Choose lean cuts like sirloin or tenderloin for best protein-to-fat ratio
Don't overcook the beef - aim for medium for optimal tenderness
Sweet potato can be batch-cooked and reheated throughout the week
Full-fat cottage cheese provides better satiety and nutrient absorption
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Nutrient-dense power bowl emphasizing vitamins and minerals alongside optimal macros for sustained energy and health.

recipes.name: Marry me butter plant-based protein pasta
instructions: Cook whole wheat pasta according to package directions, drain and set aside
Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, cook chicken until golden and cooked through (6-8 minutes)
Remove chicken, add minced garlic to pan, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Return chicken to pan, mix well
Top with grated parmesan and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Reserve pasta water to thin sauce if needed
Use full-fat Greek yogurt for creamiest texture
Butterfly chicken breast for faster cooking
Fresh spinach adds color and nutrition without overwhelming flavor
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Viral 'marry me' pasta combining butter chicken flavors with Italian comfort food. This protein-packed version keeps the indulgent taste while delivering serious nutrition.

recipes.name: Gluten-free lactose-free marry me butter plant-based protein pasta
instructions: Cook gluten-free pasta according to package directions (usually 1-2 minutes less than wheat pasta), drain and set aside
Cut chicken breast into bite-sized pieces, season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter chicken spices
Heat olive oil in pan, cook chicken until golden and cooked through (6-8 minutes)
Remove chicken, add minced garlic to pan, cook 30 seconds
Add chopped sun-dried tomatoes and remaining spice blend, cook 1 minute
Stir in lactose-free Greek yogurt and 50ml pasta water, create creamy sauce
Add spinach and cooked pasta, toss until spinach wilts
Return chicken to pan, mix well and serve immediately
recipe_tips: Gluten-free pasta can get mushy - cook al dente
Use certified gluten-free spice blends to avoid cross-contamination
Lactose-free yogurt behaves exactly like regular in this recipe
Reserve extra pasta water - gluten-free pasta absorbs more liquid
Vegetarian meat substitutes may cook faster than traditional meat
Season well as plant-based proteins absorb flavors differently
recipes.recipe_notes: Vegetarian version using plant-based meat alternatives from vegetarische slager and Beyond Meat. Gluten-free and lactose-free version of the viral 'marry me' pasta. Perfect for those with celiac disease or lactose intolerance who don't want to miss out on trending recipes.

recipes.name: Creamy mushroom spinach gnocchi
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan and sauté the chopped onion until soft
Add mashed black garlic and fry for about 2 minutes
Add half of the butter and rosemary, cook briefly on low heat, then add remaining butter
Add mushrooms and cook until golden brown and moisture has released, season with salt and pepper
Add white wine vinegar and stir, then remove half the mushrooms and the rosemary, set aside for serving
Pour cream into the pan and stir in grated pecorino
Cook gnocchi according to package instructions, reserve 60ml cooking water
Add cooking water to the sauce and cook over medium heat until slightly thickened
Add spinach in batches and cook until wilted
Add drained gnocchi to the pan and cook for a few minutes to coat in sauce
Serve topped with reserved mushrooms and extra pecorino romano
recipe_tips: Black garlic adds a deep, umami-rich flavor but regular garlic works well too
Use a mix of mushroom varieties for complex flavor (chestnut, oyster, shiitake)
Reserve some mushrooms for topping to add texture contrast
Fresh gnocchi cooks faster than dried - check package instructions
recipes.recipe_notes: Rich and comforting Italian-style gnocchi with earthy mushrooms and fresh spinach. The pecorino adds a salty, tangy finish.

recipes.name: Creamy mushroom spinach gnocchi (lactose-free)
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan and sauté the chopped onion until soft
Add mashed black garlic and fry for about 2 minutes
Add half of the butter and rosemary, cook briefly on low heat, then add remaining butter
Add mushrooms and cook until golden brown and moisture has released, season with salt and pepper
Add white wine vinegar and stir, then remove half the mushrooms and the rosemary, set aside for serving
Pour cream into the pan and stir in grated pecorino
Cook gnocchi according to package instructions, reserve 60ml cooking water
Add cooking water to the sauce and cook over medium heat until slightly thickened
Add spinach in batches and cook until wilted
Add drained gnocchi to the pan and cook for a few minutes to coat in sauce
Serve topped with reserved mushrooms and extra pecorino romano
recipe_tips: Black garlic adds a deep, umami-rich flavor but regular garlic works well too
Use a mix of mushroom varieties for complex flavor (chestnut, oyster, shiitake)
Reserve some mushrooms for topping to add texture contrast
Fresh gnocchi cooks faster than dried - check package instructions
recipes.recipe_notes: Rich and comforting Italian-style gnocchi with earthy mushrooms and fresh spinach. The pecorino adds a salty, tangy finish.

recipes.name: Chocolate peanut butter energy bars
instructions: Line an 8-inch square pan with parchment paper and lightly oil using cooking spray
Preheat oven to 180°C
In a mixing bowl, combine quick oats, cocoa powder, peanut butter, water, and vanilla extract
Stir with a rubber spatula, then lightly oil your hands and squeeze the mixture to form a dough that is firm but not crumbly
If the mixture is too crumbly, add an extra 15ml water or olive oil and knead until it forms a ball
Press the dough into the prepared pan, using oiled fingers and a flat object like a jar lid to create an even layer
Bake for 12 minutes at 180°C on the center rack
Cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a cutting board using the parchment paper
Cut the warm bar into 12 pieces with a sharp knife
Melt dark chocolate chips with olive oil in a microwave in 30-second bursts, stirring between each
Drizzle melted chocolate over the cut bars and top with whole roasted almonds if desired
Place bars in the freezer for 12 minutes to set the chocolate
recipe_tips: Use quick oats, not old-fashioned rolled oats, as they have the right texture
The dough should be firm but hold together - adjust water content as needed
Cut bars while still warm to prevent excessive crumbling
Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 1 week or freeze for longer storage
recipes.recipe_notes: Rich in plant-based protein from peanut butter and almonds. Dark chocolate provides flavanols for heart health. Makes a perfect pre- or post-workout snack.

recipes.name: Roasted sweet potato & chickpea bowl
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C
Toss sweet potato cubes with 15ml olive oil, paprika powder, salt and pepper, spread on a baking tray lined with parchment paper
Toss chickpeas with 15ml olive oil, cumin, garlic powder, salt and pepper, spread on the same tray next to the sweet potatoes
Roast for 20-25 minutes until sweet potato is soft and lightly browned and chickpeas are slightly crispy, stir once halfway
Mix tahini, 15ml olive oil, lemon juice (about 23ml from 1 lemon), and water in a small bowl until smooth, add more water if too thick
Place spinach in a bowl and top with roasted sweet potato and chickpeas
Add sliced avocado, lemon slices, and sunflower seeds
Drizzle with remaining lemon juice and tahini dressing
Adjust seasoning with extra salt and pepper if needed
recipe_tips: Stir the vegetables halfway through roasting for even cooking
Make the dressing while the vegetables are roasting to save time
Add water gradually to the tahini dressing until you reach desired consistency
Can be meal prepped - store components separately and assemble when ready to eat
recipes.recipe_notes: Nutrient-dense bowl packed with plant-based protein, healthy fats from avocado and tahini, and complex carbs from sweet potato. Rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals.

recipes.name: Hojicha chia yogurt bowl
instructions: Mix plant-based milk with hojicha powder until dissolved, or steep 1 hojicha tea bag in warm milk and let cool
Stir in chia seeds and 5ml maple syrup, refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight until thickened
Make plum sauce by combining chopped plum, 2.5ml maple syrup, lemon juice, and water in a small pan
Simmer plum mixture for 5-7 minutes until soft and syrupy, set aside to cool
Prepare buckwheat crunch by mixing raw buckwheat groats, chopped nuts, sesame seeds, 2.5ml maple syrup, and a pinch of salt
Toast the buckwheat mixture in a dry pan over medium heat for 3-5 minutes until golden and fragrant, stirring frequently
Let buckwheat crunch cool completely to become crispy
Spoon plant-based yogurt into a bowl
Top with hojicha chia pudding, plum sauce, and buckwheat crunch
Garnish with fresh fig halves and red currants
recipe_tips: Make the chia pudding the night before for a quick morning assembly
Hojicha powder gives a unique roasted tea flavor - substitute with matcha if unavailable
Buckwheat crunch can be made in larger batches and stored in an airtight container
Any seasonal berries work well in place of red currants
Use regular yogurt if plant-based is not preferred
recipes.recipe_notes: A sophisticated Japanese-inspired breakfast bowl featuring roasted green tea, chia pudding, and homemade plum sauce. Rich in omega-3s from chia seeds and antioxidants from hojicha.

recipes.name: Chocolate nut butter energy balls
instructions: Mash the banana in a mixing bowl until smooth
Add almond flour, nut butter, cinnamon, vanilla, salt, and maple syrup
Mix everything together until a sticky dough forms
Roll the mixture into 12 small balls and place on a parchment-lined tray
Chill in the refrigerator for 15 minutes to firm up
Melt the dark chocolate in a microwave in 30-second bursts, stirring between each
Roll each ball in the melted chocolate to coat
Return to the tray and chill for another 10 minutes until chocolate sets
recipe_tips: Use a ripe banana for natural sweetness and better binding
Any nut butter works - almond, peanut, or cashew
For a thinner chocolate coating, add 5ml coconut oil to the melted chocolate
Store in the refrigerator for up to 1 week
recipes.recipe_notes: These energy balls are rich in magnesium from almond flour and nut butter, making them supportive during menstrual and luteal phases. The banana provides potassium and natural sweetness.

recipes.name: Coffee tiramisu protein breakfast
instructions: In a jar or bowl, combine oats, plant-based milk, salt, cocoa powder, and chia seeds. Stir well to combine.
In a separate bowl, mix skyr with coffee-flavored protein powder until smooth. Add a splash of milk if the mixture is too thick.
Spoon the oat mixture into the bottom of a jar or bowl, then layer the protein cream on top.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight for best results.
Before serving, dust with extra cocoa powder and top with cacao nibs.
recipe_tips: If the mixture becomes too thick after chilling, stir in a splash of milk before eating
If it is too runny, add an extra teaspoon of chia seeds or reduce the milk by 20ml next time
Best prepared the night before for a quick grab-and-go breakfast
recipes.recipe_notes: A protein-rich twist on tiramisu flavors. The combination of coffee protein and cocoa creates a satisfying breakfast that feels like a treat. Oats and chia provide sustained energy.

recipes.name: Coffee caramel banana breakfast bowl
instructions: Pit the dates and blend with coconut yogurt, instant coffee, and a pinch of salt until smooth to create the coffee caramel cream.
Mash half the banana in a bowl and mix with oats, coconut milk, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt.
Spoon the oat mixture into a jar or bowl, then layer the coffee caramel cream on top.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight for best results.
Before serving, slice the remaining banana on top, add chopped hazelnuts and shaved dark chocolate.
Finish with a drizzle of olive oil and a pinch of flaky salt.

recipes.name: Miso ginger orange jackfruit tacos
instructions: Drain and shred the jackfruit into pulled texture pieces.
In a bowl, whisk together orange juice, soy sauce, miso paste, grated ginger, maple syrup, minced garlic, black pepper, and chili flakes.
Heat 10ml olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add jackfruit and cook for 3-4 minutes until slightly caramelized.
Pour the miso-orange sauce over the jackfruit and simmer for 10-15 minutes until sauce thickens and jackfruit absorbs the flavors.
Make the salsa verde: blend remaining olive oil, capers, lemon juice and zest, and parsley until chunky.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan or microwave wrapped in a damp towel.
Fill tortillas with jackfruit, top with cheese and salsa verde.
Serve immediately with extra parsley.
recipe_tips: Use young green jackfruit in brine, not the sweet ripe version
Press jackfruit dry before cooking for better caramelization
The salsa verde can be made ahead and stored for 3 days
Use vegan cheese to keep it fully plant-based
recipes.recipe_notes: A plant-based twist on miso ginger tacos. Jackfruit shreds beautifully and absorbs the umami-rich miso-orange marinade. The salsa verde adds brightness and healthy fats.

recipes.name: Miso ginger orange mushroom tacos
instructions: Slice king oyster mushrooms lengthwise into thin strips, then shred with a fork to create a pulled texture.
In a bowl, whisk together orange juice, soy sauce, miso paste, grated ginger, maple syrup, minced garlic, black pepper, and chili flakes.
Heat 10ml olive oil in a pan over high heat. Add mushrooms and cook for 5-6 minutes until golden and slightly crispy.
Pour the miso-orange sauce over the mushrooms and cook for another 5 minutes until sauce caramelizes and coats the mushrooms.
Make the salsa verde: blend remaining olive oil, capers, lemon juice and zest, and parsley until chunky.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan or microwave wrapped in a damp towel.
Fill tortillas with mushrooms, top with cheese and salsa verde.
Serve immediately with extra parsley.
recipe_tips: King oyster mushrooms have the meatiest texture - shiitake works too but has a softer bite
Shredding the mushrooms with a fork creates a pulled-meat texture
Cook mushrooms on high heat without moving for the first 2 minutes to get crispy edges
Use vegan cheese and skip for fully plant-based version
recipes.recipe_notes: Meaty king oyster mushrooms soak up the umami miso-orange glaze beautifully. The high-heat cooking creates crispy edges while the inside stays tender. Rich in B vitamins and antioxidants.

recipes.name: Crispy smoked tofu and butterbean curry
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. In a small bowl, combine chickpea flour, turmeric, garam masala, smoked paprika, cayenne, black pepper, and 1g salt.
Tear the pressed tofu into 2-3cm irregular pieces. Add to a bowl with the drained butterbeans and the spice mixture. Toss well to coat.
Spread evenly on a lined baking tray without overlapping. Bake for 18-20 minutes until crispy and golden at the edges.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a pan. Fry mustard seeds for 30 seconds until they pop. Add spring onion whites and a pinch of salt, fry 2 minutes.
Add garlic and fry 2-3 minutes, then add ginger and fry 2 minutes until the raw aroma subsides.
Add chopped chillies, coriander stalks, ground cumin and coriander. Fry 2 minutes with another pinch of salt.
Add diced tomatoes and remaining salt. Turn up heat until sizzling, then reduce to simmer. Cover and cook 8-10 minutes until tomatoes melt into the sauce.
Add 3/4 of the crispy tofu and beans to the sauce, reserving 1/4 for topping. Combine well.
Serve topped with remaining crispy tofu and beans, soy yogurt, fresh coriander, sesame seeds, and optional chilli flakes.
recipe_tips: Press the tofu well to remove excess liquid for maximum crispiness
Do not rinse the butterbeans - the starchy liquid helps the spices stick
The continuous salting at each stage builds layers of flavor
Serve with rice, naan, or roti for a complete meal
recipes.recipe_notes: A protein-packed curry with crispy baked tofu and butterbeans. The spice-coated beans and tofu get wonderfully crispy in the oven while the fresh tomato sauce stays bright and aromatic. Excellent source of plant protein and fiber.

recipes.name: Miso peanut butter banana bread
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk the ground flaxseed with warm water and set aside to thicken.
In a large mixing bowl, mash all the bananas until you have a thick pulpy texture.
Add the flax mixture, miso paste, peanut butter, maple syrup, vanilla, apple cider vinegar, and chia seeds. Mix until well combined, then add the soy milk and mix again.
Add the buckwheat flour, oats, baking powder, baking soda, and cinnamon. Mix together without overmixing, then fold in the hemp seeds and chopped dark chocolate.
The mixture should fall off your spoon in a second or two. If too stiff, add a splash more soy milk.
Preheat oven to 170°C. Pour the mix into a lined loaf tin and bake for 45-50 minutes, checking at 35 minutes. Cover with foil if the top is browning too quickly.
Allow to cool in the tin for at least an hour before slicing.
Serve toasted with extra peanut butter, berries, and a drizzle of maple syrup if desired.
recipe_tips: The bread is done when your house smells amazing - usually needs 3-5 more minutes after that
If the knife does not come out clean it might just be melted chocolate
Store at room temperature wrapped in foil for 2 days, then refrigerate for up to 4 more days
Toast slices for the best flavor and texture
recipes.recipe_notes: A nutrient-dense pre-workout snack packed with complex carbs from bananas and oats, plus protein from hemp seeds and peanut butter. The miso adds umami depth and gut-friendly probiotics. Perfect fuel before training.

recipes.name: Creamy sundried tomato orzo with tempeh (gluten-free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C.

Crumble the tempeh into small pieces onto a roasting tray. Drizzle with olive oil, tamari, garlic granules, and a pinch of salt. Toss well and flatten into an even layer. Bake for 16-18 minutes until golden and crispy at the edges.
In a large casserole pan, fry the shallot on low heat in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5-6 minutes. Add the garlic, smoked paprika, turmeric, and dried thyme. Fry for 1-2 minutes.
Add the sundried tomatoes, gluten-free orzo, and spinach. Fry for a few minutes to toast the grains, then pour in all the vegetable stock.

Bring to a bubble, then cover and reduce to simmer for 4 minutes. Remove lid, stir well, making sure nothing sticks to the bottom. Continue cooking until the stock is absorbed (about 10 minutes). The mix should be wet and creamy.
Blend the silken tofu with lemon juice until smooth. Pour over the orzo mixture, sprinkle with nutritional yeast, add the chickpeas and fresh basil. Stir together and season to taste.
Ladle into bowls and top with the roasted tempeh, plenty of black pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil.
recipe_tips: Use a wide pan so the orzo cooks evenly - if using a narrower pot, add stock gradually
The silken tofu creates a creamy sauce without any dairy
Make sure to use certified gluten-free orzo pasta
recipes.recipe_notes: A creamy, protein-packed pasta dish with crispy roasted tempeh. The silken tofu blends into a luscious dairy-free sauce, while sundried tomatoes add Mediterranean depth. This gluten-free version uses rice or corn-based orzo.

recipes.name: Shiitake mushroom miso orzotto with smoked tofu (gluten-free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C.

Press the smoked tofu to remove excess water, then crumble into small pieces onto a roasting tray. Add 3 smaller shiitake mushrooms torn in half. Drizzle with olive oil, tamari, and garlic powder. Toss well and bake for 16-18 minutes until golden.

Finely chop or pulse the remaining mushrooms in a food processor until you have pieces around 0.5cm. Fry in a large casserole pan with olive oil and a pinch of salt on medium-high heat for 5-6 minutes until the steam subsides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, fry the shallot on low heat in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5-6 minutes. Add the garlic, dried thyme, and bay leaf. Fry for 1-2 minutes.
Add all the cavolo nero and fry until it turns a brighter green. Add the gluten-free orzo and toast for a few minutes, stirring regularly.

Pour in all the vegetable stock. Bring to a bubble, cover and simmer for 4 minutes. Remove lid, stir well, making sure nothing sticks. Continue cooking until stock is absorbed. The mix should be wet and creamy. Stir in the fried mushrooms.
Blend the silken tofu with miso and lemon juice until smooth. Pour over the orzo mixture, sprinkle with nutritional yeast, and stir together. Season to taste.
Ladle into bowls and top with the roasted tofu and shiitakes. Garnish with black pepper and a drizzle of olive oil.
recipe_tips: Use a wide pan so the orzo cooks evenly - if using a narrower pot, add stock gradually
The miso adds umami depth and gut-friendly probiotics
Make sure to use certified gluten-free orzo pasta
recipes.recipe_notes: A rich, umami-packed orzotto with crispy roasted smoked tofu and shiitake mushrooms. The silken tofu and miso create a creamy, probiotic-rich sauce. This gluten-free version uses rice or corn-based orzo.

recipes.name: Tenderstem broccoli orzotto with kale miso sauce (gluten-free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Rinse the tenderstem broccoli and add to a baking tray. Season with salt and pepper, drizzle with olive oil, and bake for 15 minutes. Allow to cool, then finely chop.
In a large wide casserole dish, fry the chopped shallot on low-medium heat in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5-6 minutes. Add the minced garlic, fry 2 minutes more.

Add the gluten-free orzo and toast the grains for 2-3 minutes. Pour in the vegetable stock, bring to a bubble, then reduce to a simmer for 10-12 minutes, or 2-3 minutes less than package suggests. You want it cooked but still soupy.

Meanwhile, make the kale miso sauce. Blanch the kale in boiling water, then quickly transfer to a bowl of iced water for several minutes. Drain, then add to a blender with the miso, lemon juice, and a splash of water. Blend until smooth, season to taste.
Turn off the heat, pour the edamame into the cooked orzo, then add the kale miso sauce and stir well. Gently fold in the tahini and chopped roasted broccoli.
Serve into bowls and top with lemon zest and a little more tahini.
recipe_tips: Use a wide pan so the orzo cooks evenly - if using a narrower pot, add stock gradually
The kale miso sauce adds probiotic benefits and umami depth
Pickled onions make a great optional topping
recipes.recipe_notes: A vibrant green orzotto with roasted tenderstem broccoli and a creamy kale miso sauce. Edamame adds complete plant protein while tahini brings calcium and creaminess. This gluten-free version uses rice or corn-based orzo.

recipes.name: Spanish-style tempeh orzo with chickpeas (gluten-free)
instructions: Crumble the tempeh into small chunks and fry in a non-stick pan on medium heat with a pinch of salt and olive oil for 6-8 minutes until browning at the edges. Remove and set aside.
In a large wide casserole dish, fry the chopped shallot and red pepper on low-medium heat in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5-6 minutes. Add the minced garlic and paprika, fry 2 minutes more.

Add the diced tomatoes and fry for a few more minutes. Add the gluten-free orzo and toast the grains for a minute. Pour in the stock, bring to a bubble, then reduce to simmer for 10-12 minutes, or 2-3 minutes less than package suggests. You want it cooked but still soupy.
Turn off the heat, pour the drained chickpeas, nutritional yeast, and 2/3 of the tempeh crumbles into the cooked orzo along with the balsamic vinegar. Gently fold in and season to taste.
Serve into bowls and top with soy yogurt mixed with water for a runny texture, remaining tempeh, fresh parsley, and a drizzle of olive oil.
recipe_tips: Use a wide pan so the orzo cooks evenly - if using a narrower pot, add stock gradually
The smoked paprika gives this dish its Spanish character
Double the recipe for easy meal prep
recipes.recipe_notes: A hearty Spanish-inspired orzotto with crispy tempeh crumbles and protein-rich chickpeas. The smoked paprika and balsamic vinegar create deep, warming flavors. This gluten-free version uses rice or corn-based orzo.

recipes.name: Kale pesto pasta with tofu feta (gluten-free)
instructions: Make the tofu feta: crumble the tofu into small pieces (1-1.5cm) in a mixing bowl. Add half the lemon juice, 2 tbsp nutritional yeast, 1 crushed garlic clove, 1/2 tbsp oregano, 1 tsp olive oil, salt, black pepper, and chili flakes. Combine well and refrigerate until ready to serve.
Fry the shallot in olive oil with a pinch of salt for 5 minutes, then add the remaining crushed garlic and fry for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
Boil a full kettle of water. Cook the gluten-free pasta in plenty of salted water for 1 minute less than packet suggests.
Quick blanch the kale by popping it into a sieve and running boiling water over it, then immediately running cold water over it. Squeeze out the water.

In a blender, add the blanched kale, drained cannellini beans, remaining lemon juice and nutritional yeast, fresh basil, the fried shallot and garlic, and about 100ml pasta water. Blend until very smooth (1-2 minutes). Season to taste.
Drain the pasta (reserving some pasta water). Combine with the sauce and stir well. Serve into bowls with the tofu feta on top, more black pepper and olive oil.
recipe_tips: Reserve pasta water to loosen the sauce if it thickens too much
The tofu feta is even better if made ahead and left to marinate
Use lentil or chickpea pasta for extra protein
recipes.recipe_notes: A vibrant green pasta with creamy kale pesto and tangy tofu feta. The cannellini beans blend into the sauce for extra creaminess and protein. This gluten-free version works beautifully with rice, corn, or legume-based pasta.

recipes.name: Tofu halloumi green bean curry
instructions: Press the tofu very well until quite dry. Cut into 1.5cm cubes and add to a mixing bowl with half the lemon juice, half the olive oil, dried italian herbs, half the sea salt, chili flakes, half the nutritional yeast, and 1/3 of the maple syrup. Stir well and refrigerate while preparing the curry.
Fry the onion in olive oil with a pinch of salt on medium heat. Add the garlic and ginger, fry 2-3 minutes until the raw aroma subsides.
Add all the spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, smoked paprika, cinnamon), bay leaf, more salt, and a splash of olive oil. Fry for 1-2 minutes.
Add the chopped tomatoes, more salt, and the water. Stir well, bring to a bubble, then reduce to simmer on low heat while you fry the tofu.

Fry the marinated tofu on high heat with olive oil and a pinch of salt for 2-3 minutes each side until nicely charred. Remove from heat, pour over the remaining maple syrup, add more nutritional yeast and salt. Stir gently until absorbed.
Blanch the green beans in boiling water for 2-3 minutes, then plunge into ice water. Cut in half and add to the curry base.
Season the curry to taste, loosen with water if needed. Serve into bowls with rice, top with the tofu halloumi and toasted crushed almonds.
recipe_tips: Press the tofu very firmly for the best halloumi-like texture
The charred edges give the tofu an authentic halloumi taste
Toast the almonds in a dry pan for extra flavor
recipes.recipe_notes: A warming curry with crispy maple-glazed tofu that mimics the taste and texture of halloumi. The aromatic spice blend with garam masala and smoked paprika creates depth, while fresh green beans add crunch. Naturally gluten-free.

recipes.name: Creamy roasted cauliflower gnocchi with smoked tofu (gluten-free)
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Break the cauliflower into small florets and spread over a baking tray. Toss with olive oil, salt, and black pepper. Roast for 20 minutes until browning at the edges.
Soak the cashews and sunflower seeds in boiling water for 10 minutes, then drain.

Chop the leeks and shallot. Heat olive oil in a large sauté pan. When the shallot sizzles on contact, add all the leek and shallot with some salt. Cook on medium heat for 10 minutes until soft and the steam subsides.

Make the creamy sauce: blend the soaked cashews, sunflower seeds, nutritional yeast, 1 clove garlic, lemon juice, salt, and water until very smooth. Add more water if too thick - it should be pourable like cream.
Cook the gluten-free gnocchi according to packet instructions, drain and set aside.
Add the remaining garlic and grated smoked tofu to the leek mixture and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add half the roasted cauliflower, the cooked gnocchi, and the creamy sauce. Stir to combine.
Serve into bowls topped with remaining roasted cauliflower, fresh parsley, olive oil drizzle, and black pepper.
recipe_tips: If allergic to nuts, substitute cashews with extra sunflower seeds
The sauce should be the consistency of double cream
Look for gnocchi made with rice flour or potato only
recipes.recipe_notes: A comforting creamy gnocchi dish with roasted cauliflower and grated smoked tofu. The cashew and sunflower seed sauce creates a rich, dairy-free cream. This gluten-free version uses gnocchi made without wheat flour.

recipes.name: Butterbean puttanesca with tofu feta (gluten-free)
instructions: Cook the gluten-free pasta for 1-2 minutes less than packet instructions suggest, in very salty water.
In a medium pan on medium heat, heat olive oil then add the minced garlic and fry until fragrant (about 1 minute - do not let it burn or it will be bitter).
Add the passata, capers, caper brine, and chopped kalamata olives. Gently heat until lightly bubbling and stir everything together for 5 minutes.

Make the tofu feta: in a mixing bowl, crumble the tofu and combine with nutritional yeast, lemon juice, dried oregano, 1g salt, 1 clove garlic minced, and 7ml olive oil. Stir well and season to taste.
When the pasta is done, add it straight into the sauce with a slotted spoon. Add the butterbeans and stir well. Add the torn basil, leaving a few leaves for garnish.
Serve into bowls with the tofu feta on top, more basil, a drizzle of olive oil, and plenty of black pepper.
recipe_tips: Do not burn the garlic or the dish will taste bitter
The caper brine adds essential saltiness and depth
Use legume pasta for extra protein if desired
recipes.recipe_notes: A bold Mediterranean pasta with briny olives, capers, and tangy tofu feta. The butterbeans add creaminess and protein to this classic Italian-inspired dish. This gluten-free version uses rice, corn, or legume-based pasta.

recipes.name: Miso tofu sushi bowl
instructions: Cook the sushi rice according to packet instructions if not already prepared.
Preheat oven to 200°C.
